exercises.name: barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a classic compound exercise that primarily targets the pectoral muscles, while also engaging the triceps and shoulders. It is performed by lying on a bench and pressing a barbell up and down in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: barbell incline bench press
exercises.notes: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound upper-body exercise that targets the upper portion of the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the shoulders and triceps. It is performed on an incline bench using a barbell, making it a staple in strength training routines for developing chest size and strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell fly
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell fly is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, using dumbbells to stretch and contract the pectoral muscles through a wide arc motion.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and extend the arms straight above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement (do not lock the elbows).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, maintaining the slight elbow bend until you feel a stretch in the chest or your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the shoulder blades stable on the bench (no excessive shrugging) and avoid letting the dumbbells travel behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then exhale and bring the dumbbells back up along the same arc, focusing on squeezing the pectorals until the weights meet above the sternum.
Perform each rep with controlled tempo and avoid bouncing or using momentum; select a weight that allows full control and a safe range of motion.
exercises.description: A supine resistance exercise performed with dumbbells that isolates the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from above the sternum to the sides, producing a deep stretch and concentric squeeze of the pectorals.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench between two cable towers (about 30–45° incline).
instructions: Set an incline bench between two cable towers (about 30–45° incline). Attach single D‑handles and set the pulleys to low or just below chest height so the cables travel upward across your chest.
Sit on the bench and lie back so your head rests on the pad; grip one handle in each hand with a neutral or palms‑facing posture and feet flat on the floor.
Retract your shoulder blades and brace your core so your back stays pressed into the bench (inclined supine position). Keep a slight, fixed bend in your elbows throughout the exercise.
Start with your arms out to the sides in line with your chest (open position) and inhale, then exhale as you bring the handles together in a wide arc toward the centerline of your upper chest.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction, focusing on squeezing the pectorals and keeping the elbows slightly bent and stable (avoid driving motion from the wrists or excessive shoulder elevation).
Slowly reverse the movement on the inhale, controlling the cables as you return to the start position until you feel a stretch in the chest without losing scapular retraction.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining core engagement and avoiding excessive momentum or arching of the lower back.
exercises.description: Lying on an incline bench placed between two cable towers, you perform a bilateral fly motion with a slight elbow bend to concentrically bring the handles together and target the upper chest. The movement emphasizes a horizontal adduction arc while the core stabilizes the torso.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back (supine) on the bench while bringing the dumbbells to chest level; plant your feet flat on the floor and create a slight natural arch in your lower back.
instructions: Sit on the end of a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at your thighs.

Lie back (supine) on the bench while bringing the dumbbells to chest level; plant your feet flat on the floor and create a slight natural arch in your lower back. Retract your shoulder blades and keep them contacting the bench.
Start with the dumbbells at mid-chest, elbows bent about 90 degrees and wrists neutral (dumbbell handles aligned with forearms).
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled arc until your arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended; keep wrists stacked over elbows.

Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position, maintaining control and stopping when the dumbbells reach chest level or your elbows form ~90 degrees. Keep elbows at roughly a 30–45° angle from your torso to protect the shoulders.
Keep your core braced and feet firmly planted for stability throughout each rep; avoid excessive flaring of the ribs or letting the shoulders lift off the bench.
Use a spotter for heavy loads and choose a weight that allows controlled full-range reps with good form.
exercises.description: Flat dumbbell bench press performed lying supine on a bench, pressing two dumbbells upward from chest level to develop the pectorals; the movement also recruits the anterior delts and triceps for stability and lockout.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
Sit on the bench with the dumbbells resting on your thighs, feet flat on the floor for stability.
Use your legs to help lift the dumbbells as you lie back so the weights are beside your upper chest; hold them with a neutral or slightly supinated grip.
Retract your shoulder blades and press your upper back into the bench so your chest is proud (maintain a stable supine position on the incline).
Plant your feet and brace your core to avoid excessive arching of the spine.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together in a controlled path until your arms are almost fully extended (do not lock the elbows).
Exhale as you press up, pause briefly at the top with control, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start position at the upper chest level.
Keep the elbows at a moderate angle (not flared excessively) and the wrists neutral throughout the movement.
Repeat for desired repetitions and use a spotter for heavy sets or if you cannot safely return the weights to your thighs/stand them up.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline bench press is performed lying supine on an incline bench and pressing dumbbells upward to emphasize the upper portion of the chest. It builds strength and mass in the upper pectorals while also recruiting the front delts and triceps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and extend the arms straight above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement (do not lock the elbows).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, maintaining the slight elbow bend until you feel a stretch in the chest or your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the shoulder blades stable on the bench (no excessive shrugging) and avoid letting the dumbbells travel behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then exhale and bring the dumbbells back up along the same arc, focusing on squeezing the pectorals until the weights meet above the sternum.
Perform each rep with controlled tempo and avoid bouncing or using momentum; select a weight that allows full control and a safe range of motion.
exercises.description: A supine resistance exercise performed with dumbbells that isolates the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from above the sternum to the sides, producing a deep stretch and concentric squeeze of the pectorals.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
instructions: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
Step 2: Brace your core and gently retract and depress your shoulder blades to stabilize the shoulder girdle throughout the movement.

Step 3: Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight bend in your elbows and stopping when your upper arms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the chest (avoid dropping below bench level).

Step 4: Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulders, then exhale and drive the dumbbells back up in the same arc by contracting the pectorals until the weights meet or are close together over your sternum.
Step 5: Maintain controlled tempo (no bouncing), keep elbows fixed at the same angle, and avoid locking the arms at the top; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Lying flat on a bench while holding dumbbells, the dumbbell fly opens the arms in a wide arc and brings them together to isolate and stretch the chest (pectorals). Movement emphasizes a controlled, arcing motion with a slight bend in the elbows to protect the shoulder joint.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Smith Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench inside the Smith machine at a 30–45° angle and load the bar with appropriate weight, securing plates with collars.
instructions: Set an incline bench inside the Smith machine at a 30–45° angle and load the bar with appropriate weight, securing plates with collars.
Sit on the bench and lie back so your head, upper back, and buttocks are fully supported (supine on an incline bench); plant feet flat on the floor for stability.
Position yourself so the bar aligns with the upper chest / clavicle area at the bottom of the movement; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades lightly to create a stable pressing platform.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with palms facing away; keep wrists neutral and elbows angled about 30–45° from the torso.
Unrack the bar by rotating the bar hooks or lifting slightly and sliding it out of the safety catches, keeping the motion controlled.
Inhale and lower the bar in a slow, controlled motion to the upper chest (just below the clavicle), keeping the elbows under the bar and the forearms vertical.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward and slightly back toward the rack until arms are nearly extended (do not fully lock the elbows).
Maintain a tight core and do not over-arch the lower back; keep the head and upper back in contact with the bench throughout the set.
After the final repetition, carefully return the bar to the safety hooks by rotating it back into the rack or guiding it onto the stops.
Use safety stops or a spotter as needed and choose a controlled tempo to prioritize form and avoid shoulder strain.
exercises.description: Incline Smith bench press performed on an incline bench in a Smith machine to emphasize the upper portion of the pectorals; the fixed bar path provides stability while pressing from the upper chest toward lockout. Great for building upper chest mass with controlled, guided movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Alternate Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and choose a pair of dumbbells you can control for the desired reps.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and choose a pair of dumbbells you can control for the desired reps.
Sit on the bench and plant your feet flat on the floor; lie back so your head, upper back and hips are supported (supine on an incline).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with elbows bent (~90°), wrists stacked over the elbows and palms facing forward or slightly neutral.
Brace your core and maintain a slight natural arch in the lower back; keep shoulder blades drawn lightly together and down.
Press one dumbbell upward and slightly toward the midline until the arm is nearly extended, exhaling on the press; avoid locking the elbow.
Lower that dumbbell slowly back to the start position while the opposite arm remains paused in the lowered/shoulder-height position.
Alternate and press the other dumbbell upward in the same controlled manner, inhaling as you lower each rep.
Continue alternating reps for the set, keeping movement controlled, wrists aligned, and avoiding excessive flaring of the elbows or letting the weights drop behind the head.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this exercise presses one dumbbell at a time upward in an alternating pattern to emphasize the upper portion of the chest while improving unilateral strength and stability.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Chest Press
exercises.notes: Set the pulleys to chest height and attach single-handle grips to each side.
instructions: Set the pulleys to chest height and attach single-handle grips to each side.
Stand facing away from the machine and grab a handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-down grip; bring the handles to chest level with elbows bent and hands near the sternum.
Step forward into a staggered or shoulder-width stance so one foot is slightly in front of the other; brace your core and keep a slight bend in the knees.
Keep shoulders down and back, chest up, and press both handles forward and inward until your arms are almost fully extended (do not lock the elbows).
Exhale as you press; hold the end position briefly while keeping tension on the cables.
Inhale and slowly control the return of the handles back to the starting position at chest level, allowing the shoulder blades to set but not excessively retract.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining a stable torso and avoiding leaning forward or using excessive lower-back arching.
exercises.description: A standing cable press that targets the chest by pressing handles forward from chest level against cable resistance; the movement also engages the anterior delts and triceps while the core stabilizes the torso. Performed facing away from the cable column with a staggered stance for balance.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
instructions: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
Step 2: Brace your core and gently retract and depress your shoulder blades to stabilize the shoulder girdle throughout the movement.

Step 3: Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight bend in your elbows and stopping when your upper arms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the chest (avoid dropping below bench level).

Step 4: Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulders, then exhale and drive the dumbbells back up in the same arc by contracting the pectorals until the weights meet or are close together over your sternum.
Step 5: Maintain controlled tempo (no bouncing), keep elbows fixed at the same angle, and avoid locking the arms at the top; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Lying flat on a bench while holding dumbbells, the dumbbell fly opens the arms in a wide arc and brings them together to isolate and stretch the chest (pectorals). Movement emphasizes a controlled, arcing motion with a slight bend in the elbows to protect the shoulder joint.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench (about 30–45°) between the cable towers and attach single-handle grips to each cable; position the pulleys slightly below or near shoulder height.
instructions: Set an incline bench (about 30–45°) between the cable towers and attach single-handle grips to each cable; position the pulleys slightly below or near shoulder height.
Sit with your back pressed against the bench (leaning back on the incline), feet flat on the floor for stability, and grab one handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-facing-up grip.
Start with your arms out to the sides at about chest level and a slight bend in the elbows; shoulders should be down and retracted (not shrugged).
Brace your core and keep the torso stable (avoid twisting or leaning forward) throughout the movement.
Exhale and bring both hands together in a wide arc in front of your upper chest, leading with the elbows and maintaining the slight bend; squeeze the pectorals at the top for a brief pause.
Inhale and slowly reverse the motion, lowering the handles back out to the starting position with control until you feel a stretch in the chest.
Keep the elbows soft (do not lock) and maintain steady tension on the cables; use a weight that allows controlled reps and full range of motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining scapular stability and avoiding momentum-driven swings.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench between two cable towers, the cable incline fly works the upper portion of the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc to bring the hands together over the upper sternum. The movement uses a slight elbow bend and controlled eccentric lowering to emphasize the pectorals.

exercises.name: Cable Middle Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to about mid-chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to about mid-chest height and select an appropriate weight.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered stance (one foot slightly forward) with a slight bend in the knees and a neutral spine.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or palms-facing grip and extend your arms out to the sides so there is tension on the cables; keep a small, constant bend in the elbows.
Retract your shoulder blades and keep the shoulders down; brace your core to prevent torso movement.
Exhale and bring the handles together in front of your chest in a wide arc, leading with the elbows and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Inhale and slowly reverse the motion with control, allowing the arms to return to the starting position until you feel a gentle stretch in the chest.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, avoiding torso leaning, shoulder shrugging, or locking the elbows.
exercises.description: Standing bilateral cable fly performed with the pulleys set at mid-chest height; the exercise horizontally adducts the arms in a controlled arc to load and contract the pectorals. A staggered stance and slight elbow bend are used to stabilize and protect the joints while the chest performs the work.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so the handles are roughly at mid-chest level when seated and your back rests flat against the pad.
instructions: Adjust the seat so the handles are roughly at mid-chest level when seated and your back rests flat against the pad.
Select an appropriate weight and sit upright with feet flat on the floor, chest up and shoulders down and back.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and position your upper arms roughly parallel to the floor with a small bend in the elbows.
Start with the handles out to the sides so your chest is stretched but shoulders are not overstretched; maintain a stable torso against the back pad.
Exhale and drive the handles together in a smooth horizontal arc by contracting the pectorals, bringing your hands close together or slightly in front of the chest.
Pause briefly at peak contraction, squeezing the chest while keeping elbows slightly bent and avoiding shoulder shrugging.
Inhale and slowly reverse the movement under control, allowing the arms to return to the start position until you feel a controlled stretch in the chest.
Repeat for desired repetitions, keeping tempo controlled and avoiding leaning forward or using momentum to move the weight.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly that brings the arms together in a horizontal arc to isolate and contract the pectoral muscles. Performed on a lever/pec-deck machine with the chest supported and elbows slightly bent throughout the movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline bench press
exercises.notes: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed firmly against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward, and lift them to shoulder height.
Slowly lower the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline bench press is a compound upper body exercise that targets the upper portion of the pectoral muscles, with additional emphasis on the shoulders and triceps. It is performed on an incline bench using dumbbells, requiring stability and control throughout the movement.

exercises.name: dumbbell fly
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell fly is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, using dumbbells to stretch and contract the pectoral muscles through a wide arc motion.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Lie back on the bench and press the dumbbells up to the starting position, directly above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline fly is a chest exercise performed on an incline bench, targeting the upper pectoral muscles. It involves lowering dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc and then bringing them back together, emphasizing a stretch and contraction of the chest.

exercises.name: cable lying fly
exercises.notes: Attach the handles to the cables and lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground.
instructions: Attach the handles to the cables and lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Hold the handles with your palms facing each other and your arms extended straight above your chest.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable lying fly is a chest isolation exercise performed on a bench using cable machines. It targets the pectoral muscles and also engages the deltoids and triceps as secondary muscles. The movement involves lowering the arms in a wide arc and then bringing them back together, focusing on squeezing the chest.

exercises.name: dumbbell pullover
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head until you feel a stretch in your chest and shoulders.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
Sit with your back and head firmly against the bench, feet flat on the floor, and brace your core (tighten abs and glutes) to maintain a stable, slight arch in the lower back.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows and shoulders pulled down and back.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your upper chest/shoulder line; have a spotter if needed.
Inhale, bend the elbows, and lower the bar under control to the upper chest/clavicle area (touch point just below the top of the sternum) keeping elbows at roughly 30–60° from the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, ensuring the forearms remain vertical and the core stays braced.
Exhale and press the bar back up in a slightly backward arc until the arms are fully extended but not locked aggressively at the elbows.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then carefully re-rack the bar on the uprights with control or have the spotter assist.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and extend the arms straight above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement (do not lock the elbows).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, maintaining the slight elbow bend until you feel a stretch in the chest or your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the shoulder blades stable on the bench (no excessive shrugging) and avoid letting the dumbbells travel behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then exhale and bring the dumbbells back up along the same arc, focusing on squeezing the pectorals until the weights meet above the sternum.
Perform each rep with controlled tempo and avoid bouncing or using momentum; select a weight that allows full control and a safe range of motion.
exercises.description: A supine resistance exercise performed with dumbbells that isolates the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from above the sternum to the sides, producing a deep stretch and concentric squeeze of the pectorals.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Fly
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
instructions: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered (split) stance or feet shoulder-width apart with a slight forward lean from the hips so you have stable balance; brace your core.
Grasp a handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-down grip and take one step forward until you feel light tension in the cables; keep a small bend in the elbows throughout.
Start with your arms out to the sides roughly at shoulder height (or slightly higher for a high-cable fly) and chest lifted; retract your shoulder blades slightly to protect the joints.
Exhale and bring the handles together in a controlled arc in front of your chest at midline, leading with the elbows and squeezing the pectorals at the finish.
Pause briefly with hands meeting or slightly overlapping, then inhale and slowly resist the cables as you return your arms to the start position using the same arcing path.
Keep the movement smooth and avoid locking the elbows, excessive torso rotation, or shrugging the shoulders; maintain core tension for stability.
exercises.description: Standing cable fly performed with the pulleys set high to mid-high engages the chest by bringing the arms together in a wide arc; the shoulders and core stabilize the movement. It emphasizes the pectoral fibers with controlled concentric adduction and slow eccentric return.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles or place your forearms against the pads with a slight bend in the elbows; start with arms out to the sides and shoulder blades pulled back and down.
Exhale and drive the handles forward and together in a smooth arc by contracting the pectorals, keeping the elbows slightly bent and wrists neutral.
Pause briefly when the hands/handles meet or are directly in front of your chest, feeling the squeeze in the pectorals.
Inhale and slowly allow the levers to return to the start position under control, maintaining shoulder blade stability and avoiding rapid letting go or trunk leaning.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; use a weight that allows full control of both concentric and eccentric phases without excessive shoulder or back movement.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly performed on a lever/pec-deck: you bring the arms together in front of the chest against resistance to isolate the pectoral muscles. The movement uses a slight elbow bend and controlled arc to emphasize the inner chest.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.

instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a moderate weight.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered or shoulder-width stance with knees soft and core braced (slight forward lean at the hips).

exercises.name: barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a classic compound exercise that primarily targets the pectoral muscles, while also engaging the triceps and shoulders. It is performed by lying on a bench and pressing a barbell up and down in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell fly
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell fly is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, using dumbbells to stretch and contract the pectoral muscles through a wide arc motion.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Lie back on the bench and press the dumbbells up to the starting position, directly above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline fly is a chest exercise performed on an incline bench, targeting the upper pectoral muscles. It involves lowering dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc and then bringing them back together, emphasizing a stretch and contraction of the chest.

exercises.name: cable middle fly
exercises.notes: Attach cables to both sides of a cable machine at chest height.
instructions: Attach cables to both sides of a cable machine at chest height.
Stand in the center of the machine with one foot slightly in front of the other.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms out to the sides.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and maintain a slight forward lean.
Engage your chest muscles and bring your arms forward in a sweeping motion.
Pause for a moment at the center, then slowly return your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable middle fly is an isolation exercise targeting the pectoral muscles using a cable machine. It emphasizes chest development and also engages the deltoids and triceps as secondary muscles.

exercises.name: dumbbell around pullover
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet firmly on the ground.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet firmly on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Keeping your arms straight, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head in an arc motion.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell around pullover is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, where you lower a dumbbell behind your head in an arc and then return to the starting position. It primarily targets the pectorals, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and latissimus dorsi.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.

exercises.name: barbell decline bench press
exercises.notes: Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell decline bench press is a compound exercise targeting the lower portion of the pectoral muscles. It is performed on a decline bench, requiring the lifter to press a barbell upward from a declined position, engaging the chest, triceps, and shoulders.

exercises.name: dumbbell decline twist fly
exercises.notes: Lie down on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips.
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
As you lower the dumbbells, twist your wrists so that your palms face forward at the bottom of the movement.
Reverse the motion and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position, squeezing your chest muscles at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell decline twist fly is a chest exercise performed on a decline bench, targeting the pectoral muscles while also engaging the shoulders and triceps. The movement involves lowering dumbbells in a wide arc with a twisting motion, increasing the stretch and contraction of the chest.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Lie back on the bench and press the dumbbells up to the starting position, directly above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline fly is a chest exercise performed on an incline bench, targeting the upper pectoral muscles. It involves lowering dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc and then bringing them back together, emphasizing a stretch and contraction of the chest.

exercises.name: lever seated fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Grasp the handles with a pronated grip and keep your elbows slightly bent.
Exhale and push the handles forward, bringing them together in front of your chest.
Pause for a moment, squeezing your chest muscles.
Inhale and slowly return to the starting position, allowing your chest muscles to stretch.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever seated fly is a machine-based exercise targeting the pectoral muscles, performed by bringing the handles together in front of the chest while seated.

exercises.name: cable incline fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable machine to a low position and attach the handles.
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to a low position and attach the handles.
Sit on an incline bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, open your arms out to the sides in a controlled motion.
Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable incline fly is an isolation exercise targeting the upper chest (pectorals) using a cable machine. It involves opening and closing the arms in a fly motion while lying on an incline bench, providing constant tension throughout the movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

Lie back on the bench, keeping the dumbbell pressed against your thigh.
Using your free hand, help lift the dumbbell up to the starting position.
Hold the dumbbell directly above your shoulder with your arm extended and palm facing inward.
Lower the dumbbell out to the side in a wide arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Pause when your arm is parallel to the ground, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms and repeat.
exercises.description: The dumbbell one arm bench fly is a unilateral chest exercise that targets the pectoral muscles and also engages the shoulders. It requires balance and control to stabilize the weight with one arm while lying on a bench.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench under a rack and load the barbell; position yourself lying supine on the bench with eyes roughly under the bar.
instructions: Set up a flat bench under a rack and load the barbell; position yourself lying supine on the bench with eyes roughly under the bar.
Plant feet firmly on the floor, pull your shoulder blades together and down (retract and depress) and maintain a tight core and neutral spine.
Grip the bar with a wide, pronated grip (wider than shoulder-width) so the forearms are more angled when the bar is at chest level.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over your chest, keeping shoulders stable and the elbows under the bar.
Inhale, slowly lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest / nipple line while keeping the elbows tracking out at a moderate flare and the scapulae retracted.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar back up along a slight arc until the arms are nearly extended.
Avoid locking the elbows aggressively at the top, maintain tension in the pectorals and triceps, and keep the wrists neutral.
Return the bar to the rack with control after your set; use a spotter if lifting heavy or approaching failure.
exercises.description: A barbell bench press performed with a wider-than-shoulder grip to emphasize the outer pectoral fibers. Lying supine on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the mid-chest and pressed back to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Low Fly
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell by each knee.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell by each knee.
Lie back so your head, upper back and glutes are supported (supine on incline) and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability.
Pick up the dumbbells and bring them to your sides (low position) with palms facing each other and a slight bend in the elbows; retract the shoulder blades slightly to protect the shoulders.
Initiate the movement by squeezing the pectorals and lifting the dumbbells in a wide arc toward each other above the upper chest, keeping the same slight elbow bend throughout.
Pause briefly at the top with the dumbbells close but not forcefully smashed together; exhale during the concentric (lifting) phase.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back down along the same arc to the low starting position under control, inhaling during the descent and maintaining tension in the chest.
Keep the torso stable (avoid excessive lumbar arching or using leg drive) and limit range if you feel shoulder pain; perform all reps with controlled tempo.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while focusing on chest contraction rather than moving the weight with momentum.
exercises.description: Lying on an incline bench, perform a dumbbell fly from a low starting position to emphasize the upper chest (pectorals) by arcing the arms upward and together. Keep a small bend in the elbows and control the descent to maintain tension on the chest.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Lever Incline Single Arm Chest Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the incline seat and lever so the handle starts level with the mid to upper chest; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the incline seat and lever so the handle starts level with the mid to upper chest; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back pressed into the bench, feet flat on the floor, and the non-working hand resting on your thigh or the seat for balance.
Grasp the handle with one hand; slightly retract the shoulder blade on the working side and brace your abs to prevent torso rotation.
Exhale and press the handle upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until the arm is nearly extended (do not hyperextend the elbow).
Inhale and slowly lower the handle back to the starting chest level under control, maintaining scapular stability and an upright torso.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping hips square and avoiding twisting; switch arms and perform the same number of reps on the opposite side.
exercises.description: Seated single-arm press on an incline lever machine that emphasizes the upper chest (pectoral) on the working side while requiring shoulder and triceps assistance and core stabilization. Performed one arm at a time to isolate each side and reduce compensation.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Low Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Set a single handle on a low cable pulley (near floor level).
instructions: Step 1: Set a single handle on a low cable pulley (near floor level). Stand with the cable side to your non-working side so the line of pull is across your body.
Step 2: Take a staggered or shoulder-width stance with a slight forward lean at the hips; brace your core and keep your torso tall and stable throughout the movement.
Step 3: Grasp the handle with a neutral or slightly supinated grip and raise your arm so the elbow is slightly bent (soft elbow) and aligned roughly with the lower chest at the start.

Step 4: Exhale and pull the handle upward and across your body in a wide arc, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectoral at the end range where your hand finishes near the midline of the chest.
Step 5: Avoid excessive torso rotation or shrugging; keep the shoulder down and allow the scapula to protract slightly as you reach across to maximize chest contraction.
Step 6: Inhale and slowly return the handle along the same controlled path to the starting position, maintaining tension on the cable and control of the shoulder.
Step 7: Perform the desired reps, then switch sides; use a weight that allows full control and a smooth arc without jerking.
exercises.description: A unilateral cable fly performed with the pulley set low; the handle is pulled upward and across the body to horizontally adduct and internally rotate the shoulder, isolating the chest. This variation emphasizes the lower and inner portion of the pectoral while demanding scapular stability.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Single Arm Fly
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to mid-chest height and select a moderate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to mid-chest height and select a moderate weight.
Stand side-on to the machine with feet staggered (one foot slightly forward) for a stable base and brace your core (standing position).
Reach across and grab the handle with the working hand, palm facing in or slightly up; allow the arm to start out to the side with a small, natural bend in the elbow (not locked).
Retract the shoulder blade slightly and maintain a tall posture with shoulders down; keep the non-working arm relaxed or placed on your hip for balance.
Exhale and bring the hand across your body in a wide arc, driving horizontal adduction from the shoulder so the hand finishes in front of the chest or slightly across midline.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction while keeping the elbow slightly bent and the torso stable (avoid rotating or leaning excessively).
Inhale and slowly resist the cable as you return the arm back to the starting position with control, feeling the stretch in the pectoral at the end range.
Repeat for desired reps, then switch sides. Maintain core bracing and a fixed slight elbow bend throughout to protect the joint and target the pectorals.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable fly performs a horizontal adduction movement of the shoulder to isolate the chest (pectorals) unilaterally while the core stabilizes the torso. The exercise emphasizes controlled inward movement of the arm across the body with a slight elbow bend.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: dumbbell fly
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell fly is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, using dumbbells to stretch and contract the pectoral muscles through a wide arc motion.

exercises.name: cable middle fly
exercises.notes: Attach cables to both sides of a cable machine at chest height.
instructions: Attach cables to both sides of a cable machine at chest height.
Stand in the center of the machine with one foot slightly in front of the other.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms out to the sides.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and maintain a slight forward lean.
Engage your chest muscles and bring your arms forward in a sweeping motion.
Pause for a moment at the center, then slowly return your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable middle fly is an isolation exercise targeting the pectoral muscles using a cable machine. It emphasizes chest development and also engages the deltoids and triceps as secondary muscles.

exercises.name: cable incline fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable machine to a low position and attach the handles.
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to a low position and attach the handles.
Sit on an incline bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, open your arms out to the sides in a controlled motion.
Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable incline fly is an isolation exercise targeting the upper chest (pectorals) using a cable machine. It involves opening and closing the arms in a fly motion while lying on an incline bench, providing constant tension throughout the movement.

exercises.name: lever seated fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Grasp the handles with a pronated grip and keep your elbows slightly bent.
Exhale and push the handles forward, bringing them together in front of your chest.
Pause for a moment, squeezing your chest muscles.
Inhale and slowly return to the starting position, allowing your chest muscles to stretch.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever seated fly is a machine-based exercise targeting the pectoral muscles, performed by bringing the handles together in front of the chest while seated.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.name: barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a classic compound exercise that primarily targets the pectoral muscles, while also engaging the triceps and shoulders. It is performed by lying on a bench and pressing a barbell up and down in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline bench press
exercises.notes: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed firmly against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward, and lift them to shoulder height.
Slowly lower the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline bench press is a compound upper body exercise that targets the upper portion of the pectoral muscles, with additional emphasis on the shoulders and triceps. It is performed on an incline bench using dumbbells, requiring stability and control throughout the movement.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell fly
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell fly is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, using dumbbells to stretch and contract the pectoral muscles through a wide arc motion.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Lie back on the bench and press the dumbbells up to the starting position, directly above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline fly is a chest exercise performed on an incline bench, targeting the upper pectoral muscles. It involves lowering dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc and then bringing them back together, emphasizing a stretch and contraction of the chest.

exercises.name: cable standing fly
exercises.notes: Attach the handles to the cables at chest height.
instructions: Attach the handles to the cables at chest height.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing away from the cable machine.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing forward.
Step forward slightly to create tension in the cables.
Keep your core engaged and your back straight throughout the exercise.
With a slight bend in your elbows, slowly bring your arms forward and together in front of your chest.
Squeeze your chest muscles at the peak of the movement.
Slowly reverse the movement, returning your arms to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable standing fly is a chest isolation exercise performed using a cable machine. It targets the pectoral muscles and also engages the deltoids and triceps. The movement requires good form, balance, and control to execute safely and effectively.

exercises.name: cable lying fly
exercises.notes: Attach the handles to the cables and lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground.
instructions: Attach the handles to the cables and lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Hold the handles with your palms facing each other and your arms extended straight above your chest.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable lying fly is a chest isolation exercise performed on a bench using cable machines. It targets the pectoral muscles and also engages the deltoids and triceps as secondary muscles. The movement involves lowering the arms in a wide arc and then bringing them back together, focusing on squeezing the chest.

instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Grasp the handles with a pronated grip and keep your elbows slightly bent.
Exhale and push the handles forward, bringing them together in front of your chest.
