exercises.name: barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a classic compound exercise that primarily targets the pectoral muscles, while also engaging the triceps and shoulders. It is performed by lying on a bench and pressing a barbell up and down in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: barbell incline bench press
exercises.notes: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound upper-body exercise that targets the upper portion of the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the shoulders and triceps. It is performed on an incline bench using a barbell, making it a staple in strength training routines for developing chest size and strength.

exercises.name: barbell split squat v. 2
exercises.notes: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Take a large step forward with your right foot, keeping your torso upright.
Lower your body by bending your knees and hips until your right thigh is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat with your left leg forward for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell split squat v. 2 is a lower body strength exercise that targets the quadriceps, while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings, and calves. It involves stepping forward into a split stance and lowering the body with a barbell across the upper back.

exercises.name: dumbbell fly
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell fly is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, using dumbbells to stretch and contract the pectoral muscles through a wide arc motion.

exercises.name: dumbbell pullover
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head until you feel a stretch in your chest and shoulders.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell pullover is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, where you lower a dumbbell behind your head and then bring it back over your chest, targeting the pectorals and also engaging the lats and triceps.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: barbell wide bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows slightly flared out.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest, then push it back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell wide bench press is a compound strength exercise targeting the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the shoulders and triceps. It requires a barbell and a bench, and involves lowering the barbell to the chest with a wide grip before pressing it back up.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: barbell squat jump step rear lunge
exercises.notes: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Explode upwards, jumping off the ground as high as you can.
Land softly on your feet and immediately step back with one leg into a reverse lunge.
Lower your body down until your front thigh is parallel to the ground, keeping your back straight.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat the jump and lunge sequence on the other leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell squat jump step rear lunge is a dynamic lower body exercise that combines a squat jump with a reverse lunge, performed while holding a barbell across the upper back. This movement targets the quadriceps, glutes, hamstrings, and calves, and requires strength, coordination, and balance.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline alternate press
exercises.notes: Lean back on the bench and use your thighs to help raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Lean back on the bench and use your thighs to help raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Once at shoulder height, rotate your wrists so that the palms of your hands are facing forward.
Push the dumbbells up with your chest and shoulders, extending your arms fully.
Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating arms.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline alternate press is a chest exercise performed on an incline bench, pressing dumbbells alternately to target the upper chest, shoulders, and triceps.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Lie back on the bench and press the dumbbells up to the starting position, directly above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline fly is a chest exercise performed on an incline bench, targeting the upper pectoral muscles. It involves lowering dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc and then bringing them back together, emphasizing a stretch and contraction of the chest.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Alternate Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and choose a pair of dumbbells you can control for the desired reps.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and choose a pair of dumbbells you can control for the desired reps.
Sit on the bench and plant your feet flat on the floor; lie back so your head, upper back and hips are supported (supine on an incline).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with elbows bent (~90°), wrists stacked over the elbows and palms facing forward or slightly neutral.
Brace your core and maintain a slight natural arch in the lower back; keep shoulder blades drawn lightly together and down.
Press one dumbbell upward and slightly toward the midline until the arm is nearly extended, exhaling on the press; avoid locking the elbow.
Lower that dumbbell slowly back to the start position while the opposite arm remains paused in the lowered/shoulder-height position.
Alternate and press the other dumbbell upward in the same controlled manner, inhaling as you lower each rep.
Continue alternating reps for the set, keeping movement controlled, wrists aligned, and avoiding excessive flaring of the elbows or letting the weights drop behind the head.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this exercise presses one dumbbell at a time upward in an alternating pattern to emphasize the upper portion of the chest while improving unilateral strength and stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Press Squat
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward and a dumbbell held vertically at chest in a goblet grip (hands around the head/handle), elbows close to your body.
instructions: Start standing with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward and a dumbbell held vertically at chest in a goblet grip (hands around the head/handle), elbows close to your body.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders down and back, keep chest up and eyes forward throughout the movement.
Inhale and hinge at the hips while bending the knees to lower into a squat — push hips back and let knees track over toes until thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you rise and maintaining a strong upright torso.
Once standing, press the dumbbell overhead (or slightly forward depending on comfort), extending the elbows and engaging delts/pectorals; exhale during the press.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the goblet position at your chest with control, resetting your brace before the next descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movements steady, knees tracking toes and avoiding rounded lower back or knees collapsing inward.
exercises.description: A standing compound movement that combines a goblet-style squat with a dumbbell press; it primarily works the quads while also engaging the chest, shoulders and arms. Perform by squatting from a goblet hold and then pressing the weight as you stand.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternate Bench Press (high start)
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the lower back; hold a dumbbell in each hand with both arms fully extended above the chest (high start), wrists neutral.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the lower back; hold a dumbbell in each hand with both arms fully extended above the chest (high start), wrists neutral.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform and keep your core braced to prevent torso rotation.
Slowly lower one dumbbell under control to the mid-chest/nipple line, keeping the elbow at about a 30–45° angle from the torso; the opposite arm remains extended overhead and stable.
Inhale as you lower; pause briefly when the dumbbell reaches chest level, then exhale and press that dumbbell back up to full extension without locking the elbow aggressively.
Once the first arm is fully extended, perform the same controlled descent and press with the opposite arm, alternating reps between arms for the set.
Keep the torso still throughout (do not twist or shift hips), maintain neutral wrist alignment, and choose a weight that allows strict form and smooth, controlled single-arm movement.
exercises.description: A single-arm variation of the flat dumbbell bench press where one dumbbell is lowered and pressed at a time from a high start position to emphasize the pectorals while challenging stability. The non-working arm remains extended overhead while the working arm performs controlled reps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
Sit on the bench with the dumbbells resting on your thighs, feet flat on the floor for stability.
Use your legs to help lift the dumbbells as you lie back so the weights are beside your upper chest; hold them with a neutral or slightly supinated grip.
Retract your shoulder blades and press your upper back into the bench so your chest is proud (maintain a stable supine position on the incline).
Plant your feet and brace your core to avoid excessive arching of the spine.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together in a controlled path until your arms are almost fully extended (do not lock the elbows).
Exhale as you press up, pause briefly at the top with control, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start position at the upper chest level.
Keep the elbows at a moderate angle (not flared excessively) and the wrists neutral throughout the movement.
Repeat for desired repetitions and use a spotter for heavy sets or if you cannot safely return the weights to your thighs/stand them up.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline bench press is performed lying supine on an incline bench and pressing dumbbells upward to emphasize the upper portion of the chest. It builds strength and mass in the upper pectorals while also recruiting the front delts and triceps.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: barbell wide squat
exercises.notes: Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell wide squat is a compound lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps, with secondary emphasis on the glutes, hamstrings, and calves. It involves holding a barbell across the upper back and performing a squat with a wide stance.

exercises.name: barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a classic compound exercise that primarily targets the pectoral muscles, while also engaging the triceps and shoulders. It is performed by lying on a bench and pressing a barbell up and down in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: dumbbell goblet squat
exercises.notes: Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down into a squat position by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell vertically against your chest with both hands.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down into a squat position by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell goblet squat is a lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings, and calves. It is performed by holding a dumbbell close to the chest and squatting down, making it a great movement for building strength and improving squat technique.

exercises.name: dumbbell fly
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell fly is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, using dumbbells to stretch and contract the pectoral muscles through a wide arc motion.

exercises.name: dumbbell pullover
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head until you feel a stretch in your chest and shoulders.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell pullover is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, where you lower a dumbbell behind your head and then bring it back over your chest, targeting the pectorals and also engaging the lats and triceps.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell bent over row
exercises.notes: Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Let your arms hang straight down towards the floor, with your elbows slightly bent.
Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bent over row is a compound exercise targeting the upper back, performed by hinging at the hips and pulling dumbbells towards the chest while maintaining a straight back.

exercises.name: dumbbell squat
exercises.notes: Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell squat is a lower body strength exercise that targets the glutes, quadriceps, and hamstrings, while also engaging the calves. It is performed by holding a dumbbell in each hand and squatting down and up with proper form.

instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell bent over row
exercises.notes: Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Let your arms hang straight down towards the floor, with your elbows slightly bent.
Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bent over row is a compound exercise targeting the upper back, performed by hinging at the hips and pulling dumbbells towards the chest while maintaining a straight back.

Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the weight back to the starting position.

exercises.notes: Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down into a squat position by pushing your hips back and bending your knees. Train to muscular failure.

instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.

exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell single leg split squat
exercises.notes: Take a step forward with one foot and position your feet so that your front foot is flat on the ground and your back foot is elevated on a bench or step. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Take a step forward with one foot and position your feet so that your front foot is flat on the ground and your back foot is elevated on a bench or step.
Lower your body by bending your front knee and hip, keeping your back knee slightly bent and your back heel off the ground.
Continue lowering until your front thigh is parallel to the ground, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.
exercises.description: The dumbbell single leg split squat is a unilateral lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps, with secondary emphasis on the glutes, hamstrings, and calves. It requires balance, coordination, and strength, as one leg is elevated and the movement is performed with added resistance from dumbbells.

exercises.name: dumbbell one arm upright row
exercises.notes: Let the dumbbell hang at arm's length in front of your thighs, with your palm facing your body. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Let the dumbbell hang at arm's length in front of your thighs, with your palm facing your body.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, exhale and lift the dumbbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbow.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
exercises.description: The dumbbell one arm upright row is a unilateral shoulder exercise that targets the deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the traps and biceps. It involves lifting a dumbbell with one arm in an upright rowing motion.

exercises.name: dumbbell squat
exercises.notes: Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell squat is a lower body strength exercise that targets the glutes, quadriceps, and hamstrings, while also engaging the calves. It is performed by holding a dumbbell in each hand and squatting down and up with proper form.

exercises.name: dumbbell fly
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell fly is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, using dumbbells to stretch and contract the pectoral muscles through a wide arc motion.

exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Train to muscular failure.

Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Lower the dumbbells down towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lying triceps extension is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed while lying on a bench and extending the arms with dumbbells.

Sit upright with your chest lifted and your shoulders back, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Engage your lats and pull the handles down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever front pulldown is a machine-based exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi (lats) and is commonly used to build back strength and muscle mass. It involves pulling handles down towards the chest while seated, using a leverage machine.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: lever seated row
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and footrests to a comfortable position. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footrests to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet on the footrests.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement.
Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever seated row is a machine-based exercise targeting the upper back. It involves pulling handles towards the torso while seated, focusing on squeezing the shoulder blades together.

exercises.name: lever leg extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the machine to fit your body. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the machine to fit your body.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the footpad.
Grasp the handles or sidebars for stability.
Extend your legs forward by straightening your knees, lifting the weight.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the weight back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever leg extension is an isolation exercise performed on a leverage machine to target the quadriceps. It involves extending the knees to lift a weight, primarily working the front of the thighs.

exercises.name: lever seated leg curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to fit your body and sit on it with your back against the backrest. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the machine to fit your body and sit on it with your back against the backrest.
Place your lower legs under the padded lever, just above your ankles.
Grasp the handles on the sides of the machine for support.
Keeping your upper legs stationary, exhale and curl your legs up as far as possible.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your hamstrings.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever seated leg curl is a machine-based exercise that targets the hamstrings. It involves curling your lower legs against resistance while seated, isolating the hamstrings and minimizing involvement from other muscle groups.

exercises.name: lever standing calf raise
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to your height and stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the machine to your height and stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place your shoulders under the pads and hold onto the handles for stability.
Raise your heels as high as possible by extending your ankles.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever standing calf raise is a strength exercise performed on a leverage machine, targeting the calves. It involves raising and lowering the heels while standing, using resistance provided by the machine.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: lever incline chest press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backrest of the leverage machine to a comfortable position. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat and backrest of the leverage machine to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Push the handles forward and away from your body until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then slowly bend your elbows and lower the handles back towards your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever incline chest press is a machine-based exercise targeting the upper chest (pectorals), with secondary emphasis on the shoulders and triceps. It is performed on a leverage machine, providing guided movement for safety and stability.

exercises.name: lever reverse t-bar row
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and footplate position on the leverage machine. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footplate position on the leverage machine.
Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the footplate.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever reverse t-bar row is a machine-based exercise targeting the upper back. It involves pulling handles towards the chest while seated, emphasizing scapular retraction and upper back engagement.

exercises.name: dumbbell standing one arm reverse curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Keep your arm fully extended and close to your body, with your palm facing down.
Slowly curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
exercises.description: The dumbbell standing one arm reverse curl targets the biceps and forearms using a dumbbell with an overhand grip, emphasizing the brachialis and forearm extensors.

exercises.name: dumbbell lying femoral
exercises.notes: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and a dumbbell resting on your lower abdomen. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and a dumbbell resting on your lower abdomen.
Bend your knees and bring the dumbbell towards your glutes, keeping your feet flat on the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lying femoral exercise targets the hamstrings and also works the glutes. It involves lying on your back, bending your knees, and using a dumbbell for resistance as you bring your feet towards your glutes.

exercises.name: lever rotary calf
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so that your knees are slightly bent and your feet are flat on the footplate. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so that your knees are slightly bent and your feet are flat on the footplate.
Place your toes on the footplate, with your heels hanging off the edge.
Grasp the handles or the sides of the machine for stability.
Push through the balls of your feet, raising your heels as high as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever rotary calf exercise targets the calves using a leverage machine. It involves raising and lowering the heels while seated, focusing on the calf muscles and ankle stabilizers.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench squat
exercises.notes: Place a dumbbell on the ground in front of a bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Place a dumbbell on the ground in front of a bench.
Stand facing away from the bench with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend at the knees and hips to lower yourself down towards the bench, keeping your chest up and back straight.
Once your glutes touch the bench, push through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench squat is a lower body exercise that targets the glutes, with secondary emphasis on the quadriceps, hamstrings, and calves. It involves squatting down to a bench while holding a dumbbell, which helps guide depth and form.

exercises.name: lever alternating narrow grip seated row
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and footplate position to ensure proper alignment. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footplate position to ensure proper alignment.
Sit on the machine with your back straight and feet flat on the footplate.
Grasp the handles with a narrow grip, palms facing each other.
Keep your chest up and shoulders back throughout the exercise.
Pull one handle towards your torso while keeping the other handle stationary.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Slowly return the handle to the starting position and repeat with the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever alternating narrow grip seated row is a machine-based exercise targeting the upper back, performed by alternately pulling each handle towards the torso while maintaining proper posture.

exercises.name: dumbbell shrug
exercises.notes: Keep your arms straight and let the dumbbells hang by your sides. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Keep your arms straight and let the dumbbells hang by your sides.
Raise your shoulders as high as possible, as if you are trying to touch your ears with your shoulders.
Hold the contraction for a second, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell shrug is an isolation exercise that targets the trapezius muscles. It involves lifting the shoulders towards the ears while holding dumbbells, focusing on building strength and size in the upper back.

exercises.name: dumbbell standing reverse curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.

Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps. Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell standing reverse curl is an exercise targeting the biceps and forearms, performed by curling dumbbells with an overhand grip while standing.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated kickback
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight.
Bring your upper arms close to your sides and keep your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated kickback is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed while seated with dumbbells. It requires maintaining a stable posture and proper form to effectively engage the triceps.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the floor. Train to muscular failure.

Grasp the handles with a parallel grip, palms facing each other, and keep your arms slightly bent.
Exhale and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull the handles back and outward, away from your body.
Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever seated reverse fly (parallel grip) is a machine-based exercise targeting the rear deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the trapezius and rhomboids. It involves pulling the handles back and outward while seated, focusing on squeezing the shoulder blades together.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated triceps extension
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up overhead. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up overhead.
Bend your elbows and lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your upper arms close to your ears.
Pause for a moment, then straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated triceps extension is an isolation exercise that targets the triceps, performed while seated and using a dumbbell held with both hands overhead.

Bend your knees slightly and lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight.
Bring your upper arms close to your sides and keep your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated kickback is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed while seated with dumbbells. It requires maintaining a stable posture and proper form to effectively engage the triceps.

exercises.name: dumbbell standing biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell standing biceps curl is a classic strength exercise that targets the biceps and also engages the forearms. It involves curling dumbbells from a standing position, focusing on controlled movement and muscle contraction.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell bent over row
exercises.notes: Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Let your arms hang straight down towards the floor, with your elbows slightly bent.
Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bent over row is a compound exercise targeting the upper back, performed by hinging at the hips and pulling dumbbells towards the chest while maintaining a straight back.

exercises.name: dumbbell squat
exercises.notes: Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell squat is a lower body strength exercise that targets the glutes, quadriceps, and hamstrings, while also engaging the calves. It is performed by holding a dumbbell in each hand and squatting down and up with proper form.

exercises.name: dumbbell fly
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell fly is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, using dumbbells to stretch and contract the pectoral muscles through a wide arc motion.

exercises.name: dumbbell biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell biceps curl is a classic strength exercise that targets the biceps and also works the forearms. It involves curling dumbbells from a fully extended arm position to shoulder level, focusing on controlled movement and muscle contraction.

exercises.name: dumbbell rear lateral raise
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell rear lateral raise is an isolation exercise targeting the rear deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the traps and rhomboids. It is performed by hinging at the hips and raising dumbbells out to the sides, focusing on shoulder movement.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell bent over row
exercises.notes: Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Let your arms hang straight down towards the floor, with your elbows slightly bent.
Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bent over row is a compound exercise targeting the upper back, performed by hinging at the hips and pulling dumbbells towards the chest while maintaining a straight back.

exercises.name: lever narrow grip seated row
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and footrests to ensure proper form. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footrests to ensure proper form.
Sit on the machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the handles with a narrow grip, palms facing each other.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward.
Pull the handles towards your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement.
Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever narrow grip seated row is a machine-based exercise targeting the upper back, with secondary emphasis on the biceps and forearms. It involves pulling handles towards the torso while seated, focusing on squeezing the shoulder blades together.

exercises.name: lever leg extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the machine to fit your body. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the machine to fit your body.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the footpad.
Grasp the handles or sidebars for stability.
Extend your legs forward by straightening your knees, lifting the weight.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the weight back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever leg extension is an isolation exercise performed on a leverage machine to target the quadriceps. It involves extending the knees to lift a weight, primarily working the front of the thighs.

exercises.name: dumbbell fly
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell fly is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, using dumbbells to stretch and contract the pectoral muscles through a wide arc motion.

exercises.name: lever seated leg curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to fit your body and sit on it with your back against the backrest. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the machine to fit your body and sit on it with your back against the backrest.
Place your lower legs under the padded lever, just above your ankles.
Grasp the handles on the sides of the machine for support.
Keeping your upper legs stationary, exhale and curl your legs up as far as possible.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your hamstrings.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever seated leg curl is a machine-based exercise that targets the hamstrings. It involves curling your lower legs against resistance while seated, isolating the hamstrings and minimizing involvement from other muscle groups.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: lever deadlift
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and foot platform to your desired position. Stop 1 rep short of failure (RPE 9). Spinal safety takes priority.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and foot platform to your desired position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the foot platform.
Grasp the handles or the sides of the seat for stability.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings, and push through your heels to lift the weight up.
Keep your back straight and avoid rounding your shoulders.
Extend your hips fully at the top of the movement, squeezing your glutes.
Lower the weight back down in a controlled manner, keeping tension on your glutes and hamstrings.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever deadlift is a machine-based exercise targeting the glutes, with secondary emphasis on the hamstrings and quadriceps. It is performed on a leverage machine, providing guided movement and stability, making it accessible to a wide range of users.

exercises.name: lever front pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your knees under the pads and your feet flat on the ground. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your knees under the pads and your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Sit upright with your chest lifted and your shoulders back, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Engage your lats and pull the handles down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever front pulldown is a machine-based exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi (lats) and is commonly used to build back strength and muscle mass. It involves pulling handles down towards the chest while seated, using a leverage machine.

exercises.name: lever leg extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the machine to fit your body. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the machine to fit your body.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the footpad.
Grasp the handles or sidebars for stability.
Extend your legs forward by straightening your knees, lifting the weight.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the weight back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever leg extension is an isolation exercise performed on a leverage machine to target the quadriceps. It involves extending the knees to lift a weight, primarily working the front of the thighs.

exercises.name: lever lying leg curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to fit your body and select the desired weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the machine to fit your body and select the desired weight.
Lie face down on the machine with your legs straight and your heels against the padded lever.
Grasp the handles or the sides of the machine for stability.
Keeping your upper body stationary, exhale and curl your legs up as far as possible without lifting your hips off the pad.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your hamstrings.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever lying leg curl is an isolation exercise targeting the hamstrings, performed on a leverage machine. It involves curling the legs upward against resistance while lying face down, emphasizing hamstring contraction and control.

exercises.name: lever standing calf raise
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to your height and stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the machine to your height and stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place your shoulders under the pads and hold onto the handles for stability.
Raise your heels as high as possible by extending your ankles.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever standing calf raise is a strength exercise performed on a leverage machine, targeting the calves. It involves raising and lowering the heels while standing, using resistance provided by the machine.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench seated press
exercises.notes: Lean back and position the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, palms facing forward. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Lean back and position the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench seated press is a strength exercise targeting the deltoid muscles of the shoulders, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and chest. It is performed seated on a bench, pressing dumbbells overhead in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: lever reverse t-bar row
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and footplate position on the leverage machine. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footplate position on the leverage machine.
Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the footplate.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever reverse t-bar row is a machine-based exercise targeting the upper back. It involves pulling handles towards the chest while seated, emphasizing scapular retraction and upper back engagement.

exercises.name: dumbbell single leg split squat
exercises.notes: Take a step forward with one foot and position your feet so that your front foot is flat on the ground and your back foot is elevated on a bench or step. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Take a step forward with one foot and position your feet so that your front foot is flat on the ground and your back foot is elevated on a bench or step.
Lower your body by bending your front knee and hip, keeping your back knee slightly bent and your back heel off the ground.
Continue lowering until your front thigh is parallel to the ground, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.
exercises.description: The dumbbell single leg split squat is a unilateral lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps, with secondary emphasis on the glutes, hamstrings, and calves. It requires balance, coordination, and strength, as one leg is elevated and the movement is performed with added resistance from dumbbells.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated inner biceps curl
exercises.notes: Rest your upper arms on your thighs, allowing the dumbbells to hang down. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up.
Rest your upper arms on your thighs, allowing the dumbbells to hang down.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated inner biceps curl targets the biceps and forearms by having the lifter sit on a bench, rest their upper arms on their thighs, and curl dumbbells upward. This isolates the biceps and minimizes momentum, making it an effective strength-building exercise.

exercises.name: lever seated reverse fly (parallel grip)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the floor. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with a parallel grip, palms facing each other, and keep your arms slightly bent.
Exhale and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull the handles back and outward, away from your body.
Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever seated reverse fly (parallel grip) is a machine-based exercise targeting the rear deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the trapezius and rhomboids. It involves pulling the handles back and outward while seated, focusing on squeezing the shoulder blades together.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Grasp the handles with a narrow grip, palms facing each other.

Pull the handles towards your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement.
Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever narrow grip seated row is a machine-based exercise targeting the upper back, with secondary emphasis on the biceps and forearms. It involves pulling handles towards the torso while seated, focusing on squeezing the shoulder blades together.

exercises.name: dumbbell single leg split squat
exercises.notes: Take a step forward with one foot and position your feet so that your front foot is flat on the ground and your back foot is elevated on a bench or step. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Take a step forward with one foot and position your feet so that your front foot is flat on the ground and your back foot is elevated on a bench or step.
Lower your body by bending your front knee and hip, keeping your back knee slightly bent and your back heel off the ground.
Continue lowering until your front thigh is parallel to the ground, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.
exercises.description: The dumbbell single leg split squat is a unilateral lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps, with secondary emphasis on the glutes, hamstrings, and calves. It requires balance, coordination, and strength, as one leg is elevated and the movement is performed with added resistance from dumbbells.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated reverse grip concentration curl
exercises.notes: Rest your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing up.
Rest your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated reverse grip concentration curl is an isolation exercise targeting the biceps, performed while seated with a dumbbell and a reverse (underhand) grip. It emphasizes strict form and muscle contraction.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated curl
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated curl is a strength exercise targeting the biceps, performed while seated to minimize body movement and focus on arm isolation.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.
