exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Front Press
exercises.notes: Set up a bench with back support in front of a cable machine and attach a straight bar, V-bar, or dual handles so the cable runs to about chest/shoulder height when seated.
instructions: Set up a bench with back support in front of a cable machine and attach a straight bar, V-bar, or dual handles so the cable runs to about chest/shoulder height when seated.
Sit upright with your back firmly against the pad, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Grasp the handle(s) with a neutral or overhand grip and bring them to the front of your chest, elbows pointed down and slightly forward.
Exhale and press the handles straight out in front of you until your arms are nearly extended, keeping a small bend in the elbows and avoiding full elbow lockout.
Keep the shoulders down and avoid excessive torso lean or arching; focus on driving the movement from the delts.
Inhale and slowly return the handles under control back to the start position at chest level, maintaining tension in the cables.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and full control throughout each rep.
exercises.description: A seated cable pressing movement performed from chest level that emphasizes the anterior delts by driving the handles forward while the back is supported. It trains shoulder pressing mechanics with continuous cable tension.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
instructions: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
Lie supine on the incline bench with your head supported, feet flat on the floor, and back slightly arched while maintaining scapular retraction.
Grip the barbell with a pronated grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over your upper chest/shoulder line, then stabilize before starting the descent.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar in a controlled path to the upper chest/clavicular area, keeping elbows at roughly a 30–60° angle from the torso (avoid flaring to 90°).
Lightly touch or stop just above the chest, pause briefly without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward and slightly back to the start position, fully extending the elbows.
Keep core braced and feet planted throughout to maintain stability and prevent excessive lower back extension.
Carefully re-rack the bar under control or have a spotter assist when finished.
exercises.description: A barbell press performed on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest by pressing the bar upward and slightly toward the clavicle. It also engages the front delts and triceps for pressing and locking out the weight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.description: A barbell press performed on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest by pressing the bar upward and slightly toward the clavicle. It also engages the front delts and triceps for pressing and locking out the weight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with elbows bent ~90°, palms facing forward or slightly inward and wrists neutral.
Inhale, keep the spine neutral and shoulders down away from the ears.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid locking the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and keeping the torso stable.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Use a lighter weight or a spotter if form breaks down or if pressing heavy loads.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press is a compound pressing movement performed from a seated, back‑supported position that primarily develops the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back for stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles or place your forearms against the pads with a slight bend in the elbows; start with arms out to the sides and shoulder blades pulled back and down.
Exhale and drive the handles forward and together in a smooth arc by contracting the pectorals, keeping the elbows slightly bent and wrists neutral.
Pause briefly when the hands/handles meet or are directly in front of your chest, feeling the squeeze in the pectorals.
Inhale and slowly allow the levers to return to the start position under control, maintaining shoulder blade stability and avoiding rapid letting go or trunk leaning.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; use a weight that allows full control of both concentric and eccentric phases without excessive shoulder or back movement.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly performed on a lever/pec-deck: you bring the arms together in front of the chest against resistance to isolate the pectoral muscles. The movement uses a slight elbow bend and controlled arc to emphasize the inner chest.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a classic compound exercise that primarily targets the pectoral muscles, while also engaging the triceps and shoulders. It is performed by lying on a bench and pressing a barbell up and down in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: barbell incline bench press
exercises.notes: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound upper-body exercise that targets the upper portion of the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the shoulders and triceps. It is performed on an incline bench using a barbell, making it a staple in strength training routines for developing chest size and strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell decline bench press
exercises.notes: Lie down on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand and extend your arms straight up above your chest, palms facing forward.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell decline bench press is a chest exercise performed on a decline bench, targeting the lower pectorals. It also engages the triceps and shoulders as secondary muscles. The movement involves pressing dumbbells upward from a declined position, requiring good form and control.

exercises.name: dumbbell lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lateral raise is an isolation exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the lateral (side) head. It is commonly used to build shoulder width and strength.

exercises.name: cable pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and push the bar down until your elbows are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable pushdown is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine. It is commonly performed to build strength and size in the triceps, with some engagement of the forearms. The movement is controlled and requires proper form to maximize effectiveness and minimize risk of injury.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
Sit with your back and head firmly against the bench, feet flat on the floor, and brace your core (tighten abs and glutes) to maintain a stable, slight arch in the lower back.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows and shoulders pulled down and back.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your upper chest/shoulder line; have a spotter if needed.
Inhale, bend the elbows, and lower the bar under control to the upper chest/clavicle area (touch point just below the top of the sternum) keeping elbows at roughly 30–60° from the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, ensuring the forearms remain vertical and the core stays braced.
Exhale and press the bar back up in a slightly backward arc until the arms are fully extended but not locked aggressively at the elbows.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then carefully re-rack the bar on the uprights with control or have the spotter assist.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Place the barbell in a front-rack position resting on the upper chest/clavicle area with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and full grip around the bar.
Retract the shoulder blades slightly, keep the chest up and core braced (neutral spine); avoid excessive lumbar arching.
Take a full breath and begin the press by driving the bar upward in a straight line, slightly moving the head back as the bar passes and then returning the head forward under the bar.
Fully extend the elbows at the top with the bar directly over or slightly behind the head, shoulders stacked over hips and feet for balance.
Lower the bar under control back to the front-rack position to complete one rep while inhaling, keeping the elbows tracking slightly forward.
Repeat for desired repetitions, maintaining tight core engagement and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if you must excessively lean back to finish reps.
exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the delts while requiring the triceps and upper back to assist and stabilize. The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: barbell incline bench press
exercises.notes: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound upper-body exercise that targets the upper portion of the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the shoulders and triceps. It is performed on an incline bench using a barbell, making it a staple in strength training routines for developing chest size and strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell arnold press v. 2
exercises.notes: Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended and your palms are facing forward.
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support and hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level, palms facing your body and elbows bent.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended and your palms are facing forward.
Rotate your wrists as you lift, so that your palms end up facing forward at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell Arnold press v. 2 is a shoulder exercise that involves pressing dumbbells overhead while rotating the wrists, targeting the deltoids and engaging the triceps and upper chest.

exercises.name: dumbbell decline triceps extension
exercises.notes: Extend your arms fully, keeping your elbows close to your head.
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms fully, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Lower the dumbbells slowly behind your head, bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell decline triceps extension is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed on a decline bench to increase the stretch and activation of the triceps muscles. It also engages the shoulders as secondary muscles.

exercises.name: cable alternate triceps extension
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the cable handle with your right hand and bring your arm up so that your upper arm is parallel to the ground and your elbow is bent at a 90-degree angle.
Keep your upper arm stationary and extend your forearm backward, fully straightening your arm.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat with your left arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable alternate triceps extension is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine. It involves extending the arm at the elbow while keeping the upper arm stationary, alternating between arms.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a classic compound exercise that primarily targets the pectoral muscles, while also engaging the triceps and shoulders. It is performed by lying on a bench and pressing a barbell up and down in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: barbell decline wide-grip press
exercises.notes: Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower the barbell to your chest, keeping your elbows out to the sides.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell decline wide-grip press is a compound chest exercise performed on a decline bench using a barbell with a wide grip. It targets the lower portion of the pectoral muscles and also engages the triceps and shoulders.

exercises.name: assisted wide-grip chest dip (kneeling)
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad.
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad.
Grasp the handles with a wide grip and keep your elbows slightly bent.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The assisted wide-grip chest dip (kneeling) is a machine-based exercise targeting the pectorals, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. The assistance from the machine makes it accessible for those who may not yet have the strength for unassisted dips.

exercises.name: lever one arm shoulder press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Grasp the lever handle with one hand and position your elbow at a 90-degree angle.
Press the lever upward until your arm is fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the lever back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
exercises.description: The lever one arm shoulder press is a machine-based exercise targeting the deltoid muscles of the shoulder. It is performed one arm at a time, allowing for focused muscle engagement and unilateral strength development. The exercise also involves the triceps and upper back as secondary muscles.

exercises.name: dumbbell lying extension (across face)
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the dumbbell in an arc behind your head until your forearms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then contract your triceps to bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lying extension (across face) is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed lying on a bench while extending a dumbbell in an arc behind the head.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

workout.title: Generated Workout
workout.description: A balanced workout targeting your selected muscle groups.

exercises.name: Cable Lying Fly
instructions: Set two cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a light–moderate weight you can control through the full range.
Lie supine (face up) on a flat bench placed between the two pulley towers with feet flat on the floor for stability.
Reach down and grab one handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-facing position; start with arms out to the sides at about shoulder height and a slight bend in the elbows.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades (squeeze the shoulder blades together and down) to stabilize the shoulders before beginning each rep.

Exhale and bring both hands up and together in a wide arc over your chest, maintaining the slight elbow bend and focusing on squeezing the pectorals at the top; hands should meet or nearly meet above the sternum.

Inhale and slowly lower the handles back out to the starting position in a controlled manner until you feel a gentle stretch across the chest — do not let the elbows drop excessively behind the line of the shoulders.
Keep the torso stable and avoid arching the lower back; maintain control of the cables throughout the entire set and adjust weight if you cannot control the return.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then carefully release the handles and sit up before changing weight or leaving the bench.
exercises.description: A supine cable fly performed on a flat bench using low-set pulleys to isolate the chest by bringing the hands together in a wide arc. The movement emphasizes horizontal shoulder adduction while keeping a slight bend in the elbows to protect the joints.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Military Press (without rack)
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
Hold the bar at upper-chest / front-shoulder level (resting on front delts / clavicle area), elbows pointed slightly forward and directly under the wrists.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to stabilize the spine; keep a neutral head and avoid excessive arching of the lower back.
Inhale, then press the barbell straight up overhead by extending the elbows and driving through the delts; move the bar slightly back only enough to end with it stacked over mid-foot at full lockout.
Exhale at the top with arms fully extended but avoid hyperextending the elbows and lower back; keep the ribcage down and core tight.
Lower the bar under control back to the starting position at the upper chest, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking under the bar.
Repeat for desired reps. Use a spotter or lighter weight if not using a rack and maintain strict form to protect shoulders and spine.
exercises.description: A standing barbell press performed from the front shoulder/chest area to overhead, primarily working the shoulder delts while pressing the weight up in a vertical path. The lift requires core stabilization and arm extension to lock out overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Cable 45 degrees Reverse Fly
instructions: Set a single-handle on each high pulley and select a light-to-moderate weight. Stand facing the machine with the pulleys slightly above shoulder height.

Grab the left handle with your right hand and the right handle with your left hand (crossed grip) or hold each handle with the ipsilateral hand for a parallel setup; step back so there is tension in the cables.
Stand upright with feet shoulder-width apart, knees soft, core braced and a slight hinge at the hips so your torso is nearly vertical to about 10–20°. Keep a neutral spine and gaze forward.
Start with your hands close together in front of you at chest height and elbows slightly bent (approx. 10–20°).

Pull both handles outward and back in a wide arc at roughly a 45° angle relative to your torso, leading with the elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together at the end position so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Pause briefly at peak contraction, keeping tension on the cables and avoiding upward shrugging of the shoulders.
Slowly and controlled, return the handles to the start position while maintaining the slight elbow bend and core tension.
Repeat for desired repetitions. Focus on using the posterior delts and upper back rather than momentum or heavy shoulder shrugging.
exercises.description: Standing cable 45° reverse fly performed with high pulleys; emphasizes the posterior delts and upper-upper back by abducting the arms in a rearward, slightly angled plane while retracting the scapulae. Use a controlled motion to isolate the rear delts and avoid using momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

tips: Focus on form
Control the eccentric phase

exercises.name: barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a classic compound exercise that primarily targets the pectoral muscles, while also engaging the triceps and shoulders. It is performed by lying on a bench and pressing a barbell up and down in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: barbell incline bench press
exercises.notes: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound upper-body exercise that targets the upper portion of the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the shoulders and triceps. It is performed on an incline bench using a barbell, making it a staple in strength training routines for developing chest size and strength.

exercises.name: cable upright row
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold the cable attachment with an overhand grip.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Pull the cable attachment straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Slowly lower the cable attachment back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable upright row is a strength exercise targeting the deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the traps and biceps. It involves pulling a cable attachment upward towards the chin, leading with the elbows, to engage the shoulder muscles.

exercises.name: assisted chest dip (kneeling)
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad.
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad.
Grasp the handles with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The assisted chest dip (kneeling) is a chest-focused exercise performed on a leverage machine, where the user kneels on a pad for support. This machine-assisted variation helps reduce the load, making it accessible for those building strength or learning proper dip technique.

exercises.name: cable pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and push the bar down until your elbows are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable pushdown is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine. It is commonly performed to build strength and size in the triceps, with some engagement of the forearms. The movement is controlled and requires proper form to maximize effectiveness and minimize risk of injury.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: cable bench press
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable machine to chest height and attach the handles. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to chest height and attach the handles.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and step forward to create tension in the cables.
Position your feet firmly on the ground and engage your core.
Bend your elbows and bring your hands to shoulder level, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the handles forward, extending your arms fully in front of you.
Pause for a moment, then slowly reverse the movement, bringing your hands back to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable bench press is a chest exercise performed using a cable machine. It targets the pectoral muscles and also works the triceps and shoulders. The movement is similar to a traditional bench press but uses cables for constant tension throughout the range of motion.

exercises.name: dumbbell twisting bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
As you lower the dumbbells, twist your wrists so that your palms face towards you at the bottom of the movement.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then reverse the movement by pressing the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
As you press the dumbbells up, twist your wrists back to the starting position with palms facing away from you.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell twisting bench press is a variation of the standard dumbbell bench press that incorporates a wrist twist to further engage the triceps, chest, and shoulders. This exercise requires coordination and control to safely twist the wrists during the pressing motion.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown machine with your knees positioned under the pads.
Grasp the cable bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lean back slightly and keep your chest up, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Pull the bar down towards your upper chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable lat pulldown full range of motion is a strength exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi (lats) using a cable machine. It also engages the biceps, rhomboids, and rear deltoids. The movement involves pulling a cable bar down towards the chest while seated, emphasizing a full range of motion for optimal muscle activation.

exercises.name: dumbbell palms in incline bench press
exercises.notes: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your back against the backrest and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing inwards.
Extend your arms straight up above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower the dumbbells slowly towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then press the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell palms in incline bench press is a strength exercise targeting the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It is performed on an incline bench with dumbbells, using a palms-in grip to keep the elbows close to the body, which increases triceps activation.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell decline bench press
exercises.notes: Lie down on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand and extend your arms straight up above your chest, palms facing forward.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell decline bench press is a chest exercise performed on a decline bench, targeting the lower pectorals. It also engages the triceps and shoulders as secondary muscles. The movement involves pressing dumbbells upward from a declined position, requiring good form and control.

exercises.name: dumbbell close-grip press
exercises.notes: Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
Once at shoulder width, rotate your wrists forward so that the palms of your hands are facing away from you. This will be your starting position.
As you breathe in, slowly lower the dumbbells to your side until they are about level with your chest.
As you exhale, use your triceps to lift the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell close-grip press is an upper arm exercise that primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It is performed using dumbbells on a flat bench, focusing on pressing the weights with a close grip to maximize triceps activation.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: assisted chest dip (kneeling)
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad.
Grasp the handles with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The assisted chest dip (kneeling) is a chest-focused exercise performed on a leverage machine, where the user kneels on a pad for support. This machine-assisted variation helps reduce the load, making it accessible for those building strength or learning proper dip technique.

exercises.name: lever standing chest press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your feet flat on the ground. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands at chest level.
Push the handles forward until your arms are fully extended, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Pause for a moment, then slowly bring the handles back towards your chest, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever standing chest press is a machine-based exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the shoulders and triceps. It is performed standing, using a leverage machine, and involves pressing handles forward from chest level to full extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell standing alternate hammer curl and press
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your back straight.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell in your right hand towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Continue to raise the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the curl with your left hand.
After completing the curl with your left hand, exhale and press the dumbbell in your right hand overhead.
Extend your arm fully and hold for a moment at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the press with your left hand.
Continue alternating between curls and presses for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell standing alternate hammer curl and press is a compound exercise that targets the biceps, forearms, and shoulders. It involves curling a dumbbell with a neutral grip and then pressing it overhead, alternating arms each repetition.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Slowly lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell neutral grip bench press is a pressing exercise performed on a bench using dumbbells with palms facing each other. It primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press

instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.

exercises.name: cable bench press
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable machine to chest height and attach the handles. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to chest height and attach the handles.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and step forward to create tension in the cables.
Position your feet firmly on the ground and engage your core.
Bend your elbows and bring your hands to shoulder level, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the handles forward, extending your arms fully in front of you.
Pause for a moment, then slowly reverse the movement, bringing your hands back to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable bench press is a chest exercise performed using a cable machine. It targets the pectoral muscles and also works the triceps and shoulders. The movement is similar to a traditional bench press but uses cables for constant tension throughout the range of motion.

exercises.name: dumbbell standing alternate hammer curl and press
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your back straight.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell in your right hand towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Continue to raise the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the curl with your left hand.
After completing the curl with your left hand, exhale and press the dumbbell in your right hand overhead.
Extend your arm fully and hold for a moment at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the press with your left hand.
Continue alternating between curls and presses for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell standing alternate hammer curl and press is a compound exercise that targets the biceps, forearms, and shoulders. It involves curling a dumbbell with a neutral grip and then pressing it overhead, alternating arms each repetition.

exercises.name: lever seated dip
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so that your feet are flat on the ground and your knees are at a 90-degree angle. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so that your feet are flat on the ground and your knees are at a 90-degree angle.
Grasp the handles of the leverage machine with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Slowly lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever seated dip is a machine-based exercise targeting the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It involves pressing down on handles while seated, simulating a dip movement in a controlled environment.

exercises.name: cable pulldown (pro lat bar)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so that your thighs are parallel to the ground and your feet are flat on the floor. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so that your thighs are parallel to the ground and your feet are flat on the floor.
Grasp the lat bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Sit down and lean back slightly, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Pull the bar down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the bar back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable pulldown (pro lat bar) is a strength exercise that targets the latissimus dorsi (lats) and also works the biceps and forearms. It is performed using a cable machine with a pro lat bar attachment.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline bench press
exercises.notes: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed firmly against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward, and lift them to shoulder height.
Slowly lower the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline bench press is a compound upper body exercise that targets the upper portion of the pectoral muscles, with additional emphasis on the shoulders and triceps. It is performed on an incline bench using dumbbells, requiring stability and control throughout the movement.

exercises.name: assisted standing chin-up
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to your desired assistance level. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired assistance level.
Stand on the foot platform and grip the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your chest up and shoulders back, engage your core, and slightly bend your knees.
Pull your body up by flexing your elbows and driving your elbows down towards your sides.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The assisted standing chin-up is a machine-based exercise that helps users perform chin-ups with added support, making it easier to build strength in the lats, biceps, and forearms. The leverage machine reduces the amount of body weight lifted, allowing for proper form and gradual progression.

exercises.name: lever shoulder press v. 3
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the leverage machine to a comfortable position. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the leverage machine to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles of the machine with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Push the handles upward and forward until your arms are fully extended, but not locked.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever shoulder press v. 3 is a machine-based exercise targeting the deltoid muscles of the shoulders. It also engages the triceps and chest as secondary muscles. The movement involves pressing handles upward and forward while seated, providing a stable and controlled environment for developing shoulder strength.

exercises.name: lever overhand triceps dip
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to the appropriate height and secure your body in position. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the machine to the appropriate height and secure your body in position.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your body so that your arms are fully extended.
Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms close to your sides.
Continue lowering until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever overhand triceps dip is a machine-based exercise targeting the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It involves lowering and raising the body using a leverage machine, which provides stability and controlled resistance.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: cable seated high row (v-bar)
exercises.notes: Grasp the v-bar attachment with an overhand grip, palms facing each other, and your hands shoulder-width apart. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the cable machine with your feet flat on the floor and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the v-bar attachment with an overhand grip, palms facing each other, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from the hips.
Pull the v-bar towards your torso by retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your back muscles.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable seated high row (v-bar) is a compound exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi, performed on a cable machine using a v-bar attachment. It also engages the biceps, rhomboids, and rear deltoids, making it effective for developing upper back strength and muscle definition.

exercises.name: dumbbell twisting bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
As you lower the dumbbells, twist your wrists so that your palms face towards you at the bottom of the movement.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then reverse the movement by pressing the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
As you press the dumbbells up, twist your wrists back to the starting position with palms facing away from you.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell twisting bench press is a variation of the standard dumbbell bench press that incorporates a wrist twist to further engage the triceps, chest, and shoulders. This exercise requires coordination and control to safely twist the wrists during the pressing motion.

exercises.name: dumbbell fly
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell fly is a chest exercise performed lying on a bench, using dumbbells to stretch and contract the pectoral muscles through a wide arc motion.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Lie back on the bench and press the dumbbells up to the starting position, directly above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline fly is a chest exercise performed on an incline bench, targeting the upper pectoral muscles. It involves lowering dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc and then bringing them back together, emphasizing a stretch and contraction of the chest.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a classic upper body strength exercise that targets the pectoral muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: assisted standing chin-up
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to your desired assistance level. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired assistance level.
Stand on the foot platform and grip the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your chest up and shoulders back, engage your core, and slightly bend your knees.
Pull your body up by flexing your elbows and driving your elbows down towards your sides.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The assisted standing chin-up is a machine-based exercise that helps users perform chin-ups with added support, making it easier to build strength in the lats, biceps, and forearms. The leverage machine reduces the amount of body weight lifted, allowing for proper form and gradual progression.

exercises.name: dumbbell palms in incline bench press
exercises.notes: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your back against the backrest and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing inwards.
Extend your arms straight up above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower the dumbbells slowly towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then press the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell palms in incline bench press is a strength exercise targeting the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It is performed on an incline bench with dumbbells, using a palms-in grip to keep the elbows close to the body, which increases triceps activation.

exercises.name: cable concentration curl
exercises.notes: Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the cable handle towards your shoulder while contracting your biceps. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the floor and your knees slightly bent.
Hold the cable handle with an underhand grip and rest your elbow against the inside of your thigh.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the cable handle towards your shoulder while contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then inhale and slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
exercises.description: The cable concentration curl is an isolation exercise targeting the biceps, performed with a cable machine while seated. It emphasizes strict form and muscle contraction by bracing the upper arm against the thigh.

exercises.name: dumbbell one arm lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing your body.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Raise the dumbbell to the side, keeping your arm straight and your palm facing down.
Continue lifting until your arm is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
exercises.description: The dumbbell one arm lateral raise is an isolation exercise targeting the deltoid muscles, particularly the lateral (side) head. It also engages the trapezius and triceps as secondary muscles. This exercise helps build shoulder strength and definition, and is commonly used in strength training routines.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
