exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or slightly neutral and elbows roughly under the wrists.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain a neutral spine and prevent lower-back arching.
Exhale and press both dumbbells straight overhead in a controlled path until your arms are nearly fully extended (avoid jamming the elbows).
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows and the chin tucked slightly; do not overextend the neck or lower back at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and scapular stabilizers.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting shoulder-height position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining core tension and full control on both ascent and descent.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press that primarily works the delts by pressing weights from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, while the triceps and upper-back muscles stabilize and assist.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so the handles are roughly at shoulder height when seated and your feet can rest flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the seat so the handles are roughly at shoulder height when seated and your feet can rest flat on the floor.
Sit upright with your back and head pressed against the pad, chest up, and feet planted hip-width apart on the floor.
Grasp the machine handles with a neutral or overhand grip so your elbows are bent at about 90 degrees and hands are at shoulder level.
Brace your core, inhale, then exhale as you press the handles upward and slightly forward until your arms are nearly extended (do not fully lock the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged, then inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position at shoulder level.
Perform repetitions with a steady tempo, keeping the back pressed to the pad and avoiding excessive arching or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated lever shoulder press performed on a chest/shoulder press machine that drives the handles upward from shoulder level to extend the arms, primarily working the delts. Use a controlled motion with back supported against the pad.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: dumbbell close-grip press
exercises.notes: Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
Once at shoulder width, rotate your wrists forward so that the palms of your hands are facing away from you. This will be your starting position.
As you breathe in, slowly lower the dumbbells to your side until they are about level with your chest.
As you exhale, use your triceps to lift the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell close-grip press is an upper arm exercise that primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It is performed using dumbbells on a flat bench, focusing on pressing the weights with a close grip to maximize triceps activation.

exercises.name: dumbbell cuban press v. 2
exercises.notes: Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, press the dumbbells straight up overhead until your arms are fully extended.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with your palms facing down.
Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, press the dumbbells straight up overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Lower the dumbbells back down to shoulder height, rotating your wrists back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell Cuban press v. 2 is a shoulder exercise that targets the deltoids while also engaging the triceps and upper back. It involves a combination of pressing and rotational movement, making it effective for building strength and stability in the shoulder joint.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline curl
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Rest your upper arms on the incline bench and let your elbows hang down, fully extending your arms.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline curl is an isolation exercise targeting the biceps. Performed on an incline bench, it emphasizes the stretch and contraction of the biceps by reducing the involvement of the shoulders. This exercise is effective for building biceps strength and size.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands at shoulder level.
Push the handles upward until your arms are fully extended, but do not lock your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the handles are at shoulder height.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and bring them up to shoulder level, with your elbows bent and pointing outwards.
Press the handles upwards until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable shoulder press is a compound exercise targeting the deltoid muscles using a cable machine. It involves pressing handles overhead from shoulder height, engaging the shoulders, triceps, and upper back.

exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Slowly lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell neutral grip bench press is a pressing exercise performed on a bench using dumbbells with palms facing each other. It primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: ez-bar close-grip bench press

instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the ez barbell with a close grip, hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing forward.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Slowly lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The EZ-bar close-grip bench press is a compound upper body exercise that primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, using a close grip on an EZ barbell to emphasize triceps activation.

exercises.name: ez bar standing french press
exercises.notes: Raise the barbell above your head, fully extending your arms.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an overhand grip.
Raise the barbell above your head, fully extending your arms.
Keeping your upper arms close to your head, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The EZ bar standing French press is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed while standing and using an EZ barbell. It involves extending the arms overhead and lowering the bar behind the head, emphasizing triceps engagement.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated biceps curl to shoulder press
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your elbows close to your sides, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders.
Once your forearms are vertical, rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position by reversing the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated biceps curl to shoulder press is a compound exercise that targets the biceps and shoulders. It combines a biceps curl with a shoulder press, making it effective for building upper arm and shoulder strength.

Keep the chest up and shoulder blades stable; exhale and press the dumbbells straight up until the arms are nearly extended but not locked.

Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive lower back arching or flaring the elbows too wide.

instructions: Set the pulleys to the lowest position and attach single D-handles; select an appropriate weight on both sides.

Stand centered between the two stations with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grasp a handle in each hand with a neutral (thumb-up) grip, arms hanging down at your sides.
Cross the cables in front of you if the setup requires it so there is slight tension at the start; keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the exercise.
Brace your core and keep your torso upright (no leaning or swinging). Initiate the movement by lifting the arms out to the sides (lead with the elbows) until your hands reach about shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders down and back (do not shrug), then slowly lower the handles back to the start under control.
Repeat for the desired reps, exhaling as you lift and inhaling as you lower; avoid using momentum or excessive torso tilt to complete reps.

exercises.name: dumbbell neutral grip bench press

instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Slowly lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell neutral grip bench press is a pressing exercise performed on a bench using dumbbells with palms facing each other. It primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders.

exercises.name: dumbbell upright row
exercises.notes: Let the dumbbells hang in front of your thighs, with your arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Let the dumbbells hang in front of your thighs, with your arms fully extended.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, exhale and lift the dumbbells straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell upright row is a strength exercise targeting the deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the traps and biceps. It involves lifting dumbbells from thigh level to chin height, leading with the elbows.

exercises.name: dumbbell bicep curl lunge with bowling motion
exercises.notes: Take a step forward with your right foot, bending your right knee and lowering your body into a lunge position.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward.
Take a step forward with your right foot, bending your right knee and lowering your body into a lunge position.
As you lunge forward, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body.
At the bottom of the lunge, rotate your torso to the right, as if you were bowling a ball.
Reverse the motion, stepping back with your right foot and returning to the starting position while lowering the dumbbells back down.
Repeat the lunge and curl motion, this time rotating your torso to the left.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bicep curl lunge with bowling motion is a compound exercise that combines a forward lunge, a bicep curl, and a torso rotation, mimicking a bowling motion. This movement targets the biceps while also engaging the forearms, shoulders, core, and legs for stability and coordination.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

exercises.name: dumbbell close-grip press
exercises.notes: Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
Once at shoulder width, rotate your wrists forward so that the palms of your hands are facing away from you. This will be your starting position.
As you breathe in, slowly lower the dumbbells to your side until they are about level with your chest.
As you exhale, use your triceps to lift the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

exercises.name: dumbbell biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell biceps curl is a classic strength exercise that targets the biceps and also works the forearms. It involves curling dumbbells from a fully extended arm position to shoulder level, focusing on controlled movement and muscle contraction.

exercises.name: dumbbell lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lateral raise is an isolation exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the lateral (side) head. It is commonly used to build shoulder width and strength.

exercises.name: cable triceps pushdown (v-bar)
exercises.notes: Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
instructions: Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Engage your triceps and exhale as you push the v-bar down until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your triceps.
Inhale as you slowly return the v-bar to the starting position, maintaining control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable triceps pushdown (v-bar) is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine and a v-bar attachment. It is commonly performed to build strength and muscle definition in the triceps.

exercises.name: dumbbell reverse fly
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Extend your arms straight down in front of you, palms facing each other.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell reverse fly is an exercise that targets the rear deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the trapezius and rhomboids. It is performed by hinging at the hips, keeping the back straight, and lifting dumbbells out to the sides in a controlled manner.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows slightly in front of the body.
Keep the spine neutral and ribcage down to avoid hyperextending the lower back; squeeze the abs throughout.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders stacked over hips and the head neutral (you may slightly move your head back to allow the dumbbells to pass), keeping shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive torso lean or lower-back arching; if needed, use a lighter weight to maintain good form.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press is a compound shoulder exercise where you press dumbbells from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, primarily working the delts while engaging the triceps and upper back for support and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with elbows bent ~90°, palms facing forward or slightly inward and wrists neutral.
Inhale, keep the spine neutral and shoulders down away from the ears.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid locking the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and keeping the torso stable.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Use a lighter weight or a spotter if form breaks down or if pressing heavy loads.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press is a compound pressing movement performed from a seated, back‑supported position that primarily develops the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back for stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing inward), arms fully relaxed.
Brace your core and press your lower back lightly into the bench to maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Slightly bend your elbows (soft arms) and keep the bend constant for the entire movement.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a controlled arc until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor (shoulder height); lead with the elbows and keep wrists neutral.
Keep shoulders down (do not shrug) and avoid leaning or using torso momentum—if you need to swing, reduce the weight.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Perform repetitions with a steady tempo (e.g., 1–2 seconds up, 1–2 seconds down) and choose a weight that allows strict form.
exercises.description: A seated lateral raise isolates the lateral head of the deltoid by lifting light-to-moderate dumbbells out to the sides from a seated, supported position. Use a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to emphasize shoulder abduction and limit momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is around 30–45 degrees (or nearly parallel to the floor) with a soft bend in the knees; keep the spine neutral and neck aligned.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is around 30–45 degrees (or nearly parallel to the floor) with a soft bend in the knees; keep the spine neutral and neck aligned.
Let the dumbbells hang under your shoulders with elbows slightly bent (not locked). Brace your core to prevent swinging.

Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, leading with the elbows and keeping the slight elbow bend, until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or in line with the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the rear delts and upper back at the top for 1 second, then inhale and lower the weights slowly under control back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired reps, avoiding momentum—keep the movement slow and controlled and maintain the hip hinge throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell raise that isolates the rear (posterior) deltoids by lifting the arms out to the sides while the torso is hinged forward. Performed with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum and emphasize the rear delts and upper back.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Face Pull
exercises.notes: Set a cable pulley to about eye- to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
instructions: Set a cable pulley to about eye- to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart or in a slight staggered stance, knees soft and spine neutral.
Grasp the rope with both hands using a neutral/overhand grip (palms facing inward) and take a step back so there is tension on the cable.
Initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades and pulling the rope toward your face, driving the elbows out to the sides so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
As you reach the end range, externally rotate the shoulders so the hands split apart and the rope ends are near your temples or ears; pause and squeeze the rear delts and upper back.
Slowly reverse the motion under control, extending the arms forward while maintaining scapular control until the hands return to the start position.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping torso stable (no excessive leaning or momentum) and focusing on quality of movement and external rotation.
exercises.description: A standing cable face pull with a rope attachment that emphasizes the posterior delts and upper back by pulling the rope toward the face while externally rotating the shoulders. It develops rear shoulder strength, scapular retraction, and shoulder external rotators.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: ez barbell decline close grip face press
exercises.notes: Grasp the ez barbell with a close grip, palms facing each other.
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet.
Grasp the ez barbell with a close grip, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up above your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then press the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The EZ barbell decline close grip face press is a triceps-focused exercise performed on a decline bench using an EZ barbell. It also engages the chest and shoulders as secondary muscles. The movement requires control and proper form to avoid injury, especially due to the bar path near the face and the decline position.

exercises.name: ez barbell jm bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the ez barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower the barbell to your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in close to your body.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The EZ Barbell JM Bench Press is a triceps-focused pressing movement performed on a bench using an EZ bar. It combines elements of a close-grip bench press and a triceps extension, targeting the triceps while also engaging the chest and shoulders.

exercises.name: dumbbell lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lateral raise is an isolation exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the lateral (side) head. It is commonly used to build shoulder width and strength.

exercises.name: cable pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and push the bar down until your elbows are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable pushdown is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine. It is commonly performed to build strength and size in the triceps, with some engagement of the forearms. The movement is controlled and requires proper form to maximize effectiveness and minimize risk of injury.

exercises.name: ez barbell close-grip curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The EZ barbell close-grip curl is a biceps-focused exercise performed with an EZ barbell using a close, underhand grip. It targets the biceps and also engages the forearms as secondary muscles. The movement involves curling the barbell towards the shoulders while keeping the elbows close to the torso.

exercises.name: dumbbell biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell biceps curl is a classic strength exercise that targets the biceps and also works the forearms. It involves curling dumbbells from a fully extended arm position to shoulder level, focusing on controlled movement and muscle contraction.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Lever Military Press (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit roughly at shoulder level when seated upright; load plates and ensure the lever is securely on the start position.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit roughly at shoulder level when seated upright; load plates and ensure the lever is securely on the start position.
Sit upright with your back pressed firmly against the pad, feet flat on the floor and head in a neutral position.
Grasp the handles with a firm overhand grip, elbows bent at about 90 degrees and wrists aligned with forearms.
Brace your core and exhale as you press the handles upward in a controlled line until your arms are nearly extended (do not lock the elbows aggressively).
Pause briefly at the top while keeping shoulder blades stable, then inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive lower-back arching or thrusting the head forward.
When finished, return the lever to the rack/safe position before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever military press that performs a vertical pressing motion to develop the shoulder (delts), using a fixed lever arm for guided overhead presses. The movement also recruits the triceps to extend the elbows and the traps to stabilize the shoulder girdle.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Upright Row
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (neutral spine).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (neutral spine).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand (pronated) grip, arms extended down in front of you and weights close to your thighs.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows upward and outward, keeping the dumbbells close to the body and leading with the elbows.
Raise the dumbbells until your elbows reach roughly chest/shoulder height or just below chin level, keeping wrists relatively neutral and palms facing your body or slightly inward.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged but avoid excessive shrugging or leaning back.
Lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting position, fully extending the arms without locking the elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining an upright standing position, steady breathing, and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell upright row where you pull the weights vertically toward your upper chest, emphasizing the shoulder (deltoid) muscles with assistance from the trapezius, biceps, and forearms. Perform with a controlled motion and elbows leading to keep tension on the delts.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Cable Front Raise
exercises.notes: Step 1: Attach a straight bar or single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Step 1: Attach a straight bar or single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Step 2: Stand facing the cable machine with feet hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing upright).
Step 3: Grasp the bar with an overhand grip (palms down) about shoulder-width, arms extended down in front with a slight bend at the elbows.
Step 4: Maintain a neutral spine and keep shoulders back and down; avoid leaning or using momentum.
Step 5: Exhale and raise the bar straight forward and up with the arms (leading with the elbows) until your hands reach about shoulder height.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the top while keeping tension on the cable and the core engaged.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position under control, keeping the slight elbow bend throughout.
Step 8: Repeat for the desired reps; avoid swinging the torso or using excessive weight to maintain strict delt activation.
exercises.description: A standing cable front raise performed from a low pulley that lifts the arms straight in front of the body to shoulder height, primarily working the front portion of the shoulder. It uses a controlled movement with a slight elbow bend to isolate the anterior delts.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
instructions: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
Sit upright with your back pressed into the seat pad, feet flat on the floor, and chest up; grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly pronated grip.
Start with your elbows slightly bent and arms resting at your sides (not locked), maintaining a neutral wrist and steady grip.
Exhale and lift the handles out to your sides in a controlled arc, leading with the elbow so the upper arm reaches about parallel to the floor (shoulder height).
Keep your torso still and avoid shrugging your shoulders; use your delts to move the weight rather than momentum or back extension.
Pause briefly at the top with the elbows slightly higher than the wrists, feeling tension in the delts and traps for scapular stability.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position with control, maintaining the slight elbow bend.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Prioritize controlled tempo, full range without swinging, and maintain scapular stability throughout.
exercises.description: Seated lever lateral raise is a machine exercise that isolates the lateral head of the delts by abducting the arms out to the sides from a seated, supported position. It emphasizes controlled movement to raise the arms to about shoulder height while minimizing trunk movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Fly
exercises.notes: Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor (or a 30–45° decline), keep a neutral spine and neck, and brace your abs.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with feet flat about hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor (or a 30–45° decline), keep a neutral spine and neck, and brace your abs.
Allow the dumbbells to hang beneath your shoulders with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain that elbow angle throughout the set.

Exhale and lift the dumbbells out to your sides in a wide arc by externally rotating the shoulders and squeezing the shoulder blades together; raise until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Pause briefly at the top focusing on contracting the rear delts and upper back, then inhale and lower the weights slowly back to the starting position with control.
Avoid swinging the torso or using momentum, do not shrug the shoulders up toward the ears, and keep the movement driven by the shoulders/scapulae rather than the lower back.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell rear fly is a bent-over horizontal shoulder abduction movement that targets the rear delts and upper back by pulling the arms out to the sides while keeping the torso stable. It strengthens posterior shoulder muscles and scapular retractors with a controlled, rowing-like motion.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: dumbbell standing overhead press
exercises.notes: Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell standing overhead press is a compound exercise targeting the deltoids, performed by pressing dumbbells overhead from shoulder height while standing.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated shoulder press
exercises.notes: Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated shoulder press is a strength exercise targeting the deltoid muscles of the shoulders. It is performed while seated, pressing dumbbells overhead to build shoulder and upper arm strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell hammer curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate the palms of your hands until they are facing forward.
This will be your starting position.
Now, keeping the upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Then, inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell hammer curl is a strength exercise targeting the biceps and forearms. It is performed by curling dumbbells with a neutral grip (palms facing each other), which emphasizes the brachialis and brachioradialis muscles.

exercises.name: cable pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and push the bar down until your elbows are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable pushdown is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine. It is commonly performed to build strength and size in the triceps, with some engagement of the forearms. The movement is controlled and requires proper form to maximize effectiveness and minimize risk of injury.

exercises.name: dumbbell lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lateral raise is an isolation exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the lateral (side) head. It is commonly used to build shoulder width and strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell rear lateral raise
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell rear lateral raise is an isolation exercise targeting the rear deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the traps and rhomboids. It is performed by hinging at the hips and raising dumbbells out to the sides, focusing on shoulder movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows slightly in front of the body.
Keep the spine neutral and ribcage down to avoid hyperextending the lower back; squeeze the abs throughout.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders stacked over hips and the head neutral (you may slightly move your head back to allow the dumbbells to pass), keeping shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive torso lean or lower-back arching; if needed, use a lighter weight to maintain good form.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press is a compound shoulder exercise where you press dumbbells from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, primarily working the delts while engaging the triceps and upper back for support and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with elbows bent ~90°, palms facing forward or slightly inward and wrists neutral.
Inhale, keep the spine neutral and shoulders down away from the ears.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid locking the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and keeping the torso stable.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Use a lighter weight or a spotter if form breaks down or if pressing heavy loads.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press is a compound pressing movement performed from a seated, back‑supported position that primarily develops the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back for stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench seated press
exercises.notes: Lean back and position the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, palms facing forward.
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Lean back and position the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench seated press is a strength exercise targeting the deltoid muscles of the shoulders, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and chest. It is performed seated on a bench, pressing dumbbells overhead in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: cable kneeling rear delt row (with rope)
exercises.notes: Attach a rope handle to a low cable pulley and kneel down facing the machine.
instructions: Attach a rope handle to a low cable pulley and kneel down facing the machine.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and extend your arms fully in front of you.
Keeping your back straight and core engaged, pull the rope towards your body by retracting your shoulder blades.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement and hold for a brief pause.
Slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable kneeling rear delt row (with rope) targets the rear deltoids and upper back muscles, requiring the user to kneel and pull a rope attachment toward their body while maintaining a strong core and stable posture.

exercises.name: dumbbell biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell biceps curl is a classic strength exercise that targets the biceps and also works the forearms. It involves curling dumbbells from a fully extended arm position to shoulder level, focusing on controlled movement and muscle contraction.

exercises.name: cable two arm tricep kickback
exercises.notes: Keeping your upper arms stationary, extend your forearms backwards until your arms are fully extended.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hold the cable handle in each hand with your palms facing inwards and your arms bent at a 90-degree angle.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, extend your forearms backwards until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable two arm tricep kickback is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine. It requires maintaining proper posture and control to effectively engage the triceps while minimizing shoulder involvement.

exercises.name: dumbbell kickback
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Bring your upper arms close to your sides, with your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell kickback is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps. It involves extending the arm backward while keeping the upper arm stationary, effectively working the triceps and engaging the shoulders as secondary muscles.

exercises.name: cable seated curl
exercises.notes: Grasp the cable attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your arms fully extended.
instructions: Sit on a cable machine with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Grasp the cable attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the cable attachment towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the cable attachment back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable seated curl is an isolation exercise targeting the biceps, performed on a cable machine while seated. It emphasizes controlled movement and constant tension on the biceps throughout the range of motion.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: dumbbell standing overhead press
exercises.notes: Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell standing overhead press is a compound exercise targeting the deltoids, performed by pressing dumbbells overhead from shoulder height while standing.

exercises.name: dumbbell upright row
exercises.notes: Let the dumbbells hang in front of your thighs, with your arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Let the dumbbells hang in front of your thighs, with your arms fully extended.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, exhale and lift the dumbbells straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell upright row is a strength exercise targeting the deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the traps and biceps. It involves lifting dumbbells from thigh level to chin height, leading with the elbows.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline biceps curl
exercises.notes: Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward, and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline biceps curl is a strength exercise targeting the biceps, performed on an incline bench to increase the stretch and activation of the biceps muscle. It also engages the forearms as secondary muscles.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated preacher curl
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip, resting your upper arm against the preacher pad.
instructions: Sit on a preacher curl bench with your feet flat on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip, resting your upper arm against the preacher pad.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated preacher curl is an isolation exercise targeting the biceps, performed while seated on a preacher bench to minimize momentum and maximize biceps engagement.

exercises.name: cable pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and push the bar down until your elbows are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable pushdown is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine. It is commonly performed to build strength and size in the triceps, with some engagement of the forearms. The movement is controlled and requires proper form to maximize effectiveness and minimize risk of injury.

exercises.name: dumbbell lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lateral raise is an isolation exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the lateral (side) head. It is commonly used to build shoulder width and strength.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated biceps curl to shoulder press
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your elbows close to your sides, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders.
Once your forearms are vertical, rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position by reversing the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated biceps curl to shoulder press is a compound exercise that targets the biceps and shoulders. It combines a biceps curl with a shoulder press, making it effective for building upper arm and shoulder strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated shoulder press
exercises.notes: Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated shoulder press is a strength exercise targeting the deltoid muscles of the shoulders. It is performed while seated, pressing dumbbells overhead to build shoulder and upper arm strength.

exercises.name: ez barbell decline triceps extension
exercises.notes: Extend your arms fully, keeping your elbows close to your head.
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet and your feet secured.
Hold the ez barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms fully, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The EZ barbell decline triceps extension is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed on a decline bench using an EZ bar. It requires good control and technique to avoid injury, especially to the elbows and shoulders.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated bicep curl
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up.
Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated bicep curl is a strength exercise targeting the biceps, performed while seated to minimize body movement and focus on arm muscle engagement.

exercises.name: dumbbell biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell biceps curl is a classic strength exercise that targets the biceps and also works the forearms. It involves curling dumbbells from a fully extended arm position to shoulder level, focusing on controlled movement and muscle contraction.

exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Palms In Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip‑width apart, knees soft and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip‑width apart, knees soft and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with palms facing each other (palms‑in), elbows pointed slightly forward and wrists neutral.
Keep the torso upright and ribs down; inhale to prepare.
Exhale and press both dumbbells straight overhead in a controlled path until the arms are nearly extended but elbows are not forcibly locked.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged and scapulae stable (avoid shrugging excessively).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start position at shoulder height with control.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while keeping the core engaged and avoiding arching the lower back.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell shoulder press performed with a neutral (palms‑facing‑in) grip; primarily targets the delts while engaging the triceps and upper chest for pressing and the traps for scapular support. Performed standing to require core stability and upright posture.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell One Arm Shoulder Press (VERSION 2)
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in one hand at shoulder height with the elbow bent and the palm facing forward or slightly inward.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in one hand at shoulder height with the elbow bent and the palm facing forward or slightly inward.
Brace your core and keep a neutral spine; place the free hand on your hip or at your side for balance.
Exhale and press the dumbbell straight upward by extending the elbow and lifting the arm until it is fully or nearly fully overhead without overarching the lower back.
Keep the shoulder blade stable and avoid shrugging the working shoulder; do not lean or rotate the torso toward the working side.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell under control back to the starting shoulder-height position.
Perform the desired number of reps on one side, then switch arms and repeat. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain good posture or if the lower back arches.
exercises.description: Standing single-arm dumbbell shoulder press performed from shoulder height to overhead to target the delts. The movement uses the triceps and serratus anterior for elbow extension and scapular stability while the core resists trunk tilt.

exercises.name: Cable Front Raise
exercises.notes: Step 1: Attach a straight bar or single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Step 1: Attach a straight bar or single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Step 2: Stand facing the cable machine with feet hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing upright).
Step 3: Grasp the bar with an overhand grip (palms down) about shoulder-width, arms extended down in front with a slight bend at the elbows.
Step 4: Maintain a neutral spine and keep shoulders back and down; avoid leaning or using momentum.
Step 5: Exhale and raise the bar straight forward and up with the arms (leading with the elbows) until your hands reach about shoulder height.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the top while keeping tension on the cable and the core engaged.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position under control, keeping the slight elbow bend throughout.
Step 8: Repeat for the desired reps; avoid swinging the torso or using excessive weight to maintain strict delt activation.
exercises.description: A standing cable front raise performed from a low pulley that lifts the arms straight in front of the body to shoulder height, primarily working the front portion of the shoulder. It uses a controlled movement with a slight elbow bend to isolate the anterior delts.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Front Raise
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.

Stand upright (neutral spine) facing away from the machine with feet hip-width apart or in a slight staggered stance for stability; grasp the handle with the working hand using an overhand grip (palm facing down) and let the arm hang in front of your thigh with a small bend in the elbow.
Brace your abs, keep your chest tall and shoulders down, and hold the non-working hand on your hip or at your side to prevent torso movement.
Exhale and raise the working arm straight forward in a controlled arc until the hand reaches shoulder height (arm roughly parallel to the floor); keep the elbow slightly bent and the wrist neutral.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulder or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the arm back to the starting position under control, resisting the cable.
Perform the desired reps on one side, then switch to the other arm. Maintain strict form—no swinging or using body momentum.
exercises.description: A unilateral cable front raise that isolates the anterior deltoid by lifting a single arm straight in front of the body from a low pulley. It provides constant tension through the range of motion and requires core stabilization to prevent torso movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion
