workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: dumbbell neutral grip bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Slowly lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell neutral grip bench press is a pressing exercise performed on a bench using dumbbells with palms facing each other. It primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders.

exercises.name: cable rear delt row (with rope)
exercises.notes: Attach a rope handle to a low pulley cable machine.
instructions: Attach a rope handle to a low pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope handle with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Keep your elbows slightly bent and pull the rope towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable rear delt row (with rope) is an isolation exercise targeting the rear deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the trapezius, rhomboids, and biceps. It uses a cable machine and rope attachment to provide constant tension throughout the movement, helping to develop shoulder strength and stability.

exercises.name: cable triceps pushdown (v-bar)
exercises.notes: Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
instructions: Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Engage your triceps and exhale as you push the v-bar down until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your triceps.
Inhale as you slowly return the v-bar to the starting position, maintaining control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable triceps pushdown (v-bar) is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine and a v-bar attachment. It is commonly performed to build strength and muscle definition in the triceps.

exercises.name: cable curl
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the cable attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and curl the cable attachment towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the cable attachment back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable curl is a strength exercise targeting the biceps, performed using a cable machine. It involves curling a cable attachment towards the shoulders while keeping the elbows close to the body, providing constant tension on the biceps throughout the movement.

exercises.name: dumbbell lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lateral raise is an isolation exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the lateral (side) head. It is commonly used to build shoulder width and strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell front raise
exercises.notes: Keeping your arms straight, exhale and lift the dumbbells in front of you until they are at shoulder level.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your thighs.
Keeping your arms straight, exhale and lift the dumbbells in front of you until they are at shoulder level.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell front raise is a shoulder exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the anterior (front) portion. It also engages the biceps and trapezius as secondary muscles. The movement involves lifting dumbbells in front of the body to shoulder height, focusing on controlled motion and proper form.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: dumbbell standing overhead press
exercises.notes: Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell standing overhead press is a compound exercise targeting the deltoids, performed by pressing dumbbells overhead from shoulder height while standing.

exercises.name: dumbbell close-grip press
exercises.notes: Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
Once at shoulder width, rotate your wrists forward so that the palms of your hands are facing away from you. This will be your starting position.
As you breathe in, slowly lower the dumbbells to your side until they are about level with your chest.
As you exhale, use your triceps to lift the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell close-grip press is an upper arm exercise that primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It is performed using dumbbells on a flat bench, focusing on pressing the weights with a close grip to maximize triceps activation.

exercises.name: dumbbell biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell biceps curl is a classic strength exercise that targets the biceps and also works the forearms. It involves curling dumbbells from a fully extended arm position to shoulder level, focusing on controlled movement and muscle contraction.

exercises.name: cable pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and push the bar down until your elbows are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable pushdown is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine. It is commonly performed to build strength and size in the triceps, with some engagement of the forearms. The movement is controlled and requires proper form to maximize effectiveness and minimize risk of injury.

exercises.name: dumbbell lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lateral raise is an isolation exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the lateral (side) head. It is commonly used to build shoulder width and strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell rear lateral raise
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell rear lateral raise is an isolation exercise targeting the rear deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the traps and rhomboids. It is performed by hinging at the hips and raising dumbbells out to the sides, focusing on shoulder movement.

exercises.name: dumbbell hammer curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate the palms of your hands until they are facing forward.
This will be your starting position.
Now, keeping the upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Then, inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell hammer curl is a strength exercise targeting the biceps and forearms. It is performed by curling dumbbells with a neutral grip (palms facing each other), which emphasizes the brachialis and brachioradialis muscles.

exercises.name: dumbbell lying triceps extension
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Lower the dumbbells down towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lying triceps extension is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed while lying on a bench and extending the arms with dumbbells.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Face Pull
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is bent forward (roughly 30–60° from horizontal), keep a slight bend in the knees and maintain a neutral spine and braced core (avoid rounding the lower back).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Hinge at the hips until your torso is bent forward (roughly 30–60° from horizontal), keep a slight bend in the knees and maintain a neutral spine and braced core (avoid rounding the lower back).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders so the dumbbells are below chest level and relax the shoulder blades.
Initiate the movement by pulling the elbows up and out (away from the body) so the upper arms move roughly parallel to the floor; lead with the elbows rather than the hands.

As the elbows reach about shoulder height, externally rotate the shoulders so the dumbbells move toward the sides of your face (palms may end up facing you or slightly upward), and squeeze the rear delts and scapular muscles together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top for 1–2 seconds, keeping the torso still and chest facing the floor.
Slowly reverse the movement under control, allowing the arms to extend back to the starting hanging position while keeping the torso braced.
Breathe out during the pull and inhale as you lower; choose a weight that allows strict form and avoid using momentum or excessive torso movement.
exercises.description: A bent-over face pull performed with dumbbells that targets the rear delts by pulling the weights toward the face while hinging at the hips to emphasize posterior shoulder and upper-back activation. It combines a high-row with external rotation to hit the posterior shoulder and scapular muscles.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell One Arm Upright Row
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral grip (thumb facing forward) and the other hand resting on your hip or at your side.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral grip (thumb facing forward) and the other hand resting on your hip or at your side.
Start with the dumbbell hanging at arm's length in front of the thigh, wrist neutral and shoulder relaxed.
Exhale and pull the dumbbell straight up by leading with your elbow, keeping the elbow higher than the wrist and close to the body; aim to raise the elbow to about shoulder height.
Keep the torso vertical — avoid leaning back or using momentum from the hips; do not excessively shrug the shoulder up toward the ear.
Pause briefly at the top while squeezing the delts/traps, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the start under control.
Perform the desired number of repetitions on one side, then switch arms; maintain consistent tempo and joint positions throughout each rep.
Safety cues: stop the upward motion if you feel shoulder pain, do not raise the elbow above shoulder height to reduce impingement risk, and keep the wrist straight to avoid excessive bending.
exercises.description: Standing one-arm upright row: lift a single dumbbell by driving the elbow upward and out to emphasize the lateral delts and upper traps while keeping the torso upright. Perform controlled raises to about shoulder height and lower under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternate Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core (abs) braced to keep the torso stable.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core (abs) braced to keep the torso stable.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward, elbows bent and aligned slightly in front of the body.
Press one dumbbell straight overhead by extending the elbow and driving through the shoulder until the arm is nearly straight (avoid locking the elbow); exhale during the press.
Lower that dumbbell under control back to shoulder height while inhaling.
Immediately press the other dumbbell overhead in the same controlled manner, alternating arms with each repetition.
Keep the ribcage down and avoid excessive arching of the lower back; use the traps to stabilize the shoulders and maintain a neutral head position throughout.
Perform the movement in a slow, controlled rhythm and choose a weight that allows full range of motion without compensatory torso lean.
exercises.description: A standing alternating dumbbell shoulder press where one dumbbell is pressed overhead at a time; primarily develops the delts while the triceps assist and the traps and core stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Front Raise
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.

Stand upright (neutral spine) facing away from the machine with feet hip-width apart or in a slight staggered stance for stability; grasp the handle with the working hand using an overhand grip (palm facing down) and let the arm hang in front of your thigh with a small bend in the elbow.
Brace your abs, keep your chest tall and shoulders down, and hold the non-working hand on your hip or at your side to prevent torso movement.
Exhale and raise the working arm straight forward in a controlled arc until the hand reaches shoulder height (arm roughly parallel to the floor); keep the elbow slightly bent and the wrist neutral.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulder or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the arm back to the starting position under control, resisting the cable.
Perform the desired reps on one side, then switch to the other arm. Maintain strict form—no swinging or using body momentum.
exercises.description: A unilateral cable front raise that isolates the anterior deltoid by lifting a single arm straight in front of the body from a low pulley. It provides constant tension through the range of motion and requires core stabilization to prevent torso movement.

exercises.name: Cable Y raise
exercises.notes: Set two low pulleys (near the floor) and attach single D-handles; select a light–moderate weight.
instructions: Set two low pulleys (near the floor) and attach single D-handles; select a light–moderate weight.
Stand between or slightly in front of the pulleys with feet hip-width apart, core braced and a slight bend in the knees.
Grasp the handles with a neutral grip (thumbs pointing up) so the cables cross in front of your body, arms hanging down.
Begin the movement by raising your arms diagonally and outward in a Y shape (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) with a small bend in the elbows.
Continue lifting until your hands are roughly overhead or slightly in front of you, keeping the shoulders down and scapulae rotating upward (avoid excessive shrugging).
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts and scapular stabilizers engage, then lower the handles slowly and under control back to the start.
Maintain a stable torso throughout—avoid leaning back or using momentum; use a lighter weight if you must swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: A standing cable variation where you pull the handles upward and outward in a Y-shaped path to emphasize the shoulder delts and scapular upward rotation. It trains the delts while engaging the traps and serratus anterior to stabilize and rotate the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Bent Arm Lateral raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (standing, upright position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (standing, upright position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing in) and elbows bent about 90 degrees, dumbbells resting by your hips.
Keep a slight forward torso angle if comfortable, chest up and neck neutral; do not lean or arch the lower back.
Exhale and raise your forearms out to the sides by driving the elbows upward, leading with the elbows so the dumbbells travel in an arc to shoulder height (upper arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top while keeping the elbow bend constant and maintaining scapular control (avoid shrugging the shoulders).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control, maintaining the bent elbow angle throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping movement strict—avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: A standing lateral raise variation performed with elbows bent to reduce lever length and emphasize the lateral delts; dumbbells are lifted out to the sides with a neutral grip until the upper arms reach roughly parallel to the floor.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: lever shoulder press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the backrest.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the backrest.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands at shoulder level.
Push the handles upward until your arms are fully extended, but do not lock your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever shoulder press is a machine-based exercise targeting the deltoid muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and chest. It is performed using a leverage machine, providing guided movement for safety and stability.

exercises.name: dumbbell scott press
exercises.notes: Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell Scott press is a shoulder exercise performed while seated, pressing dumbbells overhead to target the deltoid muscles.

exercises.name: dumbbell lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lateral raise is an isolation exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the lateral (side) head. It is commonly used to build shoulder width and strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell front raise
exercises.notes: Keeping your arms straight, exhale and lift the dumbbells in front of you until they are at shoulder level.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your thighs.
Keeping your arms straight, exhale and lift the dumbbells in front of you until they are at shoulder level.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell front raise is a shoulder exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the anterior (front) portion. It also engages the biceps and trapezius as secondary muscles. The movement involves lifting dumbbells in front of the body to shoulder height, focusing on controlled motion and proper form.

exercises.name: ez barbell curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale as you curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The EZ barbell curl is a classic arm exercise that targets the biceps using an EZ curl bar. It is performed standing, with an underhand grip, and involves curling the bar up towards the shoulders while keeping the elbows close to the torso.

exercises.name: dumbbell biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell biceps curl is a classic strength exercise that targets the biceps and also works the forearms. It involves curling dumbbells from a fully extended arm position to shoulder level, focusing on controlled movement and muscle contraction.

exercises.name: cable triceps pushdown (v-bar)
exercises.notes: Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
instructions: Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Engage your triceps and exhale as you push the v-bar down until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your triceps.
Inhale as you slowly return the v-bar to the starting position, maintaining control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable triceps pushdown (v-bar) is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine and a v-bar attachment. It is commonly performed to build strength and muscle definition in the triceps.

exercises.name: dumbbell kickback
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Bring your upper arms close to your sides, with your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell kickback is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps. It involves extending the arm backward while keeping the upper arm stationary, effectively working the triceps and engaging the shoulders as secondary muscles.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: lever shoulder press
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Front Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a pronated (palms down) or neutral grip at your sides.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Fly
exercises.notes: Sit near the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Bent Arm Lateral raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Lateral Raise (VERSION 2)
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle attachment on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.

exercises.name: Cable High Pull with Rope Attachment
exercises.notes: Set a cable machine to the lowest pulley and attach a rope.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Front Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a pronated (palms down) or neutral grip at your sides.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a pronated (palms down) or neutral grip at your sides.
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and relax the shoulders; brace your core to prevent leaning or using momentum.
Exhale and raise both dumbbells forward in a smooth arc until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor (shoulder height), keeping the wrists neutral and elbows fixed at the slight bend.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining shoulder tension; avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled tempo, resisting gravity.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; use light-to-moderate weight to maintain strict form and avoid swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell front raises isolate the anterior delts by lifting light dumbbells straight in front of the body while seated with back support, raising the arms to about shoulder height. The movement emphasizes shoulder flexion with controlled tempo and minimal torso movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows slightly in front of the body.
Keep the spine neutral and ribcage down to avoid hyperextending the lower back; squeeze the abs throughout.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders stacked over hips and the head neutral (you may slightly move your head back to allow the dumbbells to pass), keeping shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive torso lean or lower-back arching; if needed, use a lighter weight to maintain good form.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press is a compound shoulder exercise where you press dumbbells from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, primarily working the delts while engaging the triceps and upper back for support and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Fly
exercises.notes: Sit near the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart.
instructions: Sit near the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart.

Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is leaning forward (near-parallel to the floor) while keeping a neutral spine and neck; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip and arms hanging down.
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and brace your core to prevent swinging.

Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, leading with your elbows and focusing on contracting the rear delts; lift until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or in line with your shoulders.
Pause briefly at the top while squeezing the shoulder blades, then slowly lower the weights back to the start under control while inhaling.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a neutral spine and avoiding momentum or torso rotation.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell fly that emphasizes the rear deltoids by horizontally abducting the arms from a forward-leaning seated position. Performed with a slight elbow bend while squeezing the shoulder blades at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Bent Arm Lateral raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Bend your elbows to about 90 degrees so the dumbbells are in front of your thighs and palms face down or slightly toward each other.
Keeping the elbows bent and the torso still, raise your forearms and elbows out to the sides until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor (elbows stay higher than the hands).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining shoulder blade stability; avoid excessive shrugging or leaning the torso.
Slowly lower the weights back to the start under control, keeping tension on the delts and avoiding swinging.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining neutral spine and steady breathing throughout.
exercises.description: A standing lateral raise variation performed with the elbows bent to shorten the lever arm and focus tension on the lateral delts. Lift the dumbbells out to the sides until the upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor, then lower with control.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Lateral Raise (VERSION 2)
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle attachment on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single-handle attachment on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand upright side-on to the machine with feet shoulder-width apart; grasp the handle with the hand farthest from the machine using a neutral grip (thumb slightly up).
Place your free hand lightly on the machine for balance and keep a slight bend in the working elbow throughout; maintain an upright spine and braced core.
Start with the working arm hanging down at your side and scapula held neutral (no shrugging).
Exhale and raise the arm laterally away from the body to about shoulder height, leading with the elbow and keeping the wrist in line with the forearm; avoid leaning or swinging the torso.
Pause briefly at the top with tension on the delts, then inhale and lower the arm slowly and under control back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Maintain controlled tempo and avoid excessive weight that causes shoulder shrugging or torso momentum.
exercises.description: A single-arm lateral raise performed with a low cable to isolate the lateral deltoid; standing next to the machine provides constant tension through the movement while the other hand steadies the torso. Use a slight elbow bend and lift the arm out to shoulder height, then lower slowly.

exercises.name: Cable High Pull with Rope Attachment
exercises.notes: Set a cable machine to the lowest pulley and attach a rope.
instructions: Set a cable machine to the lowest pulley and attach a rope. Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder‑width apart and the cable between your legs.
Grasp the rope with a neutral (thumbs up) grip, hands about shoulder width apart, and take a small stagger or athletic stance with knees slightly bent and torso upright (not rounded).
Begin with the rope held near mid‑thigh, brace your core, and pull explosively by driving your elbows up and out, keeping the hands close together until the elbows reach chest/face height.

At the top of the movement, spread the rope ends slightly apart and think of lifting the elbows higher than the hands to emphasize the delts and traps; keep the chest up and shoulder blades slightly retracted.
Lower the rope under control back to the start position, resisting the weight (do not let it snap down).
Repeat for the desired reps while maintaining a neutral spine, minimal torso lean, and controlled breathing (exhale on the pull, inhale on the return).
exercises.description: Standing cable high pull with a rope attachment — pull the rope from a low pulley upward and outward, leading with the elbows to target the shoulder deltoids while also engaging the upper traps and upper back.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.notes: Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.

exercises.notes: Press one dumbbell overhead, fully extending your arm.

exercises.name: dumbbell biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.

exercises.name: cable pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.

exercises.name: dumbbell hammer curl v. 2
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.

exercises.name: cable pushdown (with rope attachment)
exercises.notes: Attach a rope attachment to a high pulley on a cable machine.

exercises.name: dumbbell lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.

exercises.name: dumbbell rear lateral raise
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged.

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated shoulder press
exercises.notes: Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated shoulder press is a strength exercise targeting the deltoid muscles of the shoulders. It is performed while seated, pressing dumbbells overhead to build shoulder and upper arm strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell standing alternate overhead press
exercises.notes: Press one dumbbell overhead, fully extending your arm.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Press one dumbbell overhead, fully extending your arm.
Lower the dumbbell back to shoulder level.
Repeat with the other arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell standing alternate overhead press is a shoulder exercise that involves pressing dumbbells overhead one arm at a time while standing. This movement targets the deltoid muscles and also engages the triceps and core for stability.

exercises.name: dumbbell biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell biceps curl is a classic strength exercise that targets the biceps and also works the forearms. It involves curling dumbbells from a fully extended arm position to shoulder level, focusing on controlled movement and muscle contraction.

exercises.name: cable pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and push the bar down until your elbows are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable pushdown is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine. It is commonly performed to build strength and size in the triceps, with some engagement of the forearms. The movement is controlled and requires proper form to maximize effectiveness and minimize risk of injury.

exercises.name: dumbbell hammer curl v. 2
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate the palms of your hands until they are facing forward.
This will be your starting position.
Now, keeping the upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Then, inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell hammer curl v. 2 is a biceps-focused exercise performed with dumbbells, emphasizing the brachialis and forearm muscles. It is performed standing, with palms facing the torso, and involves curling the weights while keeping the upper arms stationary.

exercises.name: cable pushdown (with rope attachment)
exercises.notes: Attach a rope attachment to a high pulley on a cable machine.
instructions: Attach a rope attachment to a high pulley on a cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the rope with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Exhale and push the rope downward by extending your elbows until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position by allowing your elbows to flex.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable pushdown (with rope attachment) is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps. It is performed using a cable machine with a rope attachment, focusing on extending the elbows to work the triceps while also engaging the forearms as secondary muscles.

exercises.name: dumbbell lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lateral raise is an isolation exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the lateral (side) head. It is commonly used to build shoulder width and strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell rear lateral raise
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell rear lateral raise is an isolation exercise targeting the rear deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the traps and rhomboids. It is performed by hinging at the hips and raising dumbbells out to the sides, focusing on shoulder movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated shoulder press
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.notes: Stand hip-width with a slight bend in the knees and hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.

exercises.name: Cable Front Raise (rope attachment)
exercises.notes: Set the cable to the lowest position and attach a rope.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Face Pull (with rope)
exercises.notes: Set a rope handle on a high pulley (about head/eye level) and select an appropriate weight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.

exercises.name: Cable Y raise
exercises.notes: Set two low pulleys (near the floor) and attach single D-handles; select a light–moderate weight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Reverse Butterfly
exercises.notes: Stand hip-width with a slight bend in the knees and hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
instructions: Stand hip-width with a slight bend in the knees and hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).

Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is tilted forward (about 30–45 degrees), keeping the spine neutral and neck long; let the dumbbells hang in front of your chest with elbows bent ~90° and dumbbells close together.
Brace your abs and maintain a stable torso; do not rotate or arch your lower back during the movement.

Exhale and open your elbows out to the sides by squeezing your rear delts and pulling your shoulder blades together, moving the dumbbells outward and slightly back until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or in line with your torso.
Pause briefly at the top and feel the contraction between the shoulder blades.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control, keeping the elbow bend and avoiding momentum.
Avoid shrugging (do not elevate the shoulders) and keep the motion driven by the posterior delts and upper back rather than the forearms or lower back.
exercises.description: A standing, bent-over reverse fly variation performed with dumbbells to target the rear delts and upper back by horizontally abducting the arms while keeping the torso hinged. Performed with a slight forward lean and controlled scapular retraction to emphasize posterior shoulder muscles.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand upright next to the machine with the working side closest to the pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and core braced.
Grasp the handle with a neutral grip (thumb slightly forward) and allow the arm to hang down at your side with a small bend in the elbow.
Keep your torso stable (no rotation) and, if needed, take a small step away from the machine to create tension in the cable.
Exhale and raise the arm out to the side in a controlled arc until the hand reaches about shoulder height (elbow stays slightly bent); avoid shrugging the shoulder.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulder engaged, then inhale and slowly lower the arm back to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps on one arm, then switch sides. Maintain steady tempo and avoid using momentum or leaning the torso to lift the weight.
exercises.description: A single-arm lateral raise performed with a low cable to target the lateral head of the deltoid; the cable provides continuous tension through the movement. Performed standing, the arm is lifted out to the side to about shoulder height with a slight elbow bend.

exercises.name: Cable Front Raise (rope attachment)
exercises.notes: Set the cable to the lowest position and attach a rope.
instructions: Set the cable to the lowest position and attach a rope. Stand facing away from the weight stack (facing forward) with the rope between your legs.

Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced. Grasp the rope ends with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) holding the rope in front of your thighs; maintain a slight bend in the elbows.

Keep your torso stationary and shoulders down; exhale and lift the rope straight forward and up by flexing at the shoulder until your forearms are roughly parallel to the floor (shoulder height). Lead the movement with the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top and focus on contracting the front of the delts; keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Inhale and lower the rope slowly and under control back to the starting position, maintaining the slight elbow bend and core bracing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Use a weight that allows controlled motion—avoid swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Standing cable front raises with a rope attachment target the anterior deltoids by lifting the rope straight in front of the body from a low pulley; the movement uses a controlled shoulder flexion with a slight elbow bend. Core and grip muscles assist to stabilize the torso and handle the load.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Face Pull (with rope)
exercises.notes: Set a rope handle on a high pulley (about head/eye level) and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a rope handle on a high pulley (about head/eye level) and select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright facing the machine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent (or seated on a bench); keep a tall spine and neutral head position.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (thumbs pointing toward you) and extend your arms so there is tension on the cable.
Initiate the movement by pulling the rope toward your face, leading with your elbows and keeping them at about shoulder height.
As you pull, externally rotate your shoulders so the ends of the rope separate and your hands move either side of your face; squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end range.
Pause briefly at peak contraction, keeping the torso stable and avoiding excessive backward lean or shrugging.
Slowly return the rope to the start position with control, maintaining tension in the cable and scapular control throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of reps and maintain controlled tempo to protect the shoulder joint.
exercises.description: A seated cable face pull using a rope attachment that emphasizes the rear delts and upper posterior shoulder musculature by pulling the rope toward the face while externally rotating the hands and squeezing the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing inward), arms fully relaxed.
Brace your core and press your lower back lightly into the bench to maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Slightly bend your elbows (soft arms) and keep the bend constant for the entire movement.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a controlled arc until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor (shoulder height); lead with the elbows and keep wrists neutral.
Keep shoulders down (do not shrug) and avoid leaning or using torso momentum—if you need to swing, reduce the weight.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Perform repetitions with a steady tempo (e.g., 1–2 seconds up, 1–2 seconds down) and choose a weight that allows strict form.
exercises.description: A seated lateral raise isolates the lateral head of the deltoid by lifting light-to-moderate dumbbells out to the sides from a seated, supported position. Use a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to emphasize shoulder abduction and limit momentum.

exercises.name: Cable Y raise
exercises.notes: Set two low pulleys (near the floor) and attach single D-handles; select a light–moderate weight.
instructions: Set two low pulleys (near the floor) and attach single D-handles; select a light–moderate weight.
Stand between or slightly in front of the pulleys with feet hip-width apart, core braced and a slight bend in the knees.
Grasp the handles with a neutral grip (thumbs pointing up) so the cables cross in front of your body, arms hanging down.
Begin the movement by raising your arms diagonally and outward in a Y shape (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) with a small bend in the elbows.
Continue lifting until your hands are roughly overhead or slightly in front of you, keeping the shoulders down and scapulae rotating upward (avoid excessive shrugging).
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts and scapular stabilizers engage, then lower the handles slowly and under control back to the start.
Maintain a stable torso throughout—avoid leaning back or using momentum; use a lighter weight if you must swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: A standing cable variation where you pull the handles upward and outward in a Y-shaped path to emphasize the shoulder delts and scapular upward rotation. It trains the delts while engaging the traps and serratus anterior to stabilize and rotate the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Reverse Butterfly
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.notes: Lean back and position the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, palms facing forward.

exercises.name: ez-bar close-grip bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.

exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to the appropriate height and secure your body in position.

exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.

exercises.name: dumbbell biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.

exercises.name: dumbbell zottman curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.

exercises.name: dumbbell rear delt raise
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated alternate shoulder

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: dumbbell bench seated press
exercises.notes: Lean back and position the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, palms facing forward.
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Lean back and position the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench seated press is a strength exercise targeting the deltoid muscles of the shoulders, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and chest. It is performed seated on a bench, pressing dumbbells overhead in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: ez-bar close-grip bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the ez barbell with a close grip, hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing forward.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Slowly lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The EZ-bar close-grip bench press is a compound upper body exercise that primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, using a close grip on an EZ barbell to emphasize triceps activation.

exercises.name: lever overhand triceps dip
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine to the appropriate height and secure your body in position.
instructions: Adjust the machine to the appropriate height and secure your body in position.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your body so that your arms are fully extended.
Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms close to your sides.
Continue lowering until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever overhand triceps dip is a machine-based exercise targeting the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It involves lowering and raising the body using a leverage machine, which provides stability and controlled resistance.

exercises.name: dumbbell alternate seated hammer curl
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso and arms extended down.
Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell in your right hand towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Continue to raise the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement with your left arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell alternate seated hammer curl is an upper arm exercise that targets the biceps and forearms. It is performed while seated, alternating curls with each arm using a neutral grip.

exercises.name: dumbbell biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell biceps curl is a classic strength exercise that targets the biceps and also works the forearms. It involves curling dumbbells from a fully extended arm position to shoulder level, focusing on controlled movement and muscle contraction.

exercises.name: dumbbell zottman curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate your palms to face forward.
Curl the dumbbells up to your shoulders while keeping your upper arms stationary.
At the top of the movement, rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing away from your body.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, rotating your palms back to facing your body.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell Zottman curl is a biceps exercise that incorporates both a standard curl and a reverse curl, targeting the biceps and forearms. It involves rotating the wrists at the top of the movement to work both muscle groups effectively.

exercises.name: dumbbell rear delt raise
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell rear delt raise is an isolation exercise targeting the rear deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the trapezius and rhomboids. It is performed by hinging at the hips and raising dumbbells out to the sides, focusing on shoulder movement.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated alternate shoulder
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards, and raise them to shoulder height.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards, and raise them to shoulder height.
Press one dumbbell up overhead while keeping the other dumbbell at shoulder height.
Lower the raised dumbbell back to shoulder height while simultaneously pressing the other dumbbell up overhead.
Continue alternating between arms for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated alternate shoulder press is a strength exercise targeting the deltoids, performed while seated and alternating arms for each press. It also engages the triceps and upper back as secondary muscles.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
