workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: lever military press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the backrest.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the backrest.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Push the handles upward until your arms are fully extended, but do not lock your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever military press is a shoulder exercise performed on a leverage machine, primarily targeting the deltoid muscles. It also engages the triceps and upper chest as secondary muscles. The machine provides stability, making it suitable for those developing strength and technique.

exercises.name: lever shoulder press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the backrest.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the backrest.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands at shoulder level.
Push the handles upward until your arms are fully extended, but do not lock your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The lever shoulder press is a machine-based exercise targeting the deltoid muscles, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and chest. It is performed using a leverage machine, providing guided movement for safety and stability.

exercises.name: dumbbell incline curl
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Rest your upper arms on the incline bench and let your elbows hang down, fully extending your arms.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline curl is an isolation exercise targeting the biceps. Performed on an incline bench, it emphasizes the stretch and contraction of the biceps by reducing the involvement of the shoulders. This exercise is effective for building biceps strength and size.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated preacher curl
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip, resting your upper arm against the preacher pad.
instructions: Sit on a preacher curl bench with your feet flat on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip, resting your upper arm against the preacher pad.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated preacher curl is an isolation exercise targeting the biceps, performed while seated on a preacher bench to minimize momentum and maximize biceps engagement.

exercises.name: dumbbell lying supine biceps curl
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your back and head supported, and your feet flat on the ground.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your back and head supported, and your feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing up and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lying supine biceps curl is an isolation exercise targeting the biceps, performed while lying flat on a bench. It emphasizes strict form and minimizes cheating by stabilizing the upper body.

exercises.name: cable rope high pulley overhead tricep extension
exercises.notes: Attach a rope to a high pulley and adjust the weight accordingly.
instructions: Attach a rope to a high pulley and adjust the weight accordingly.
Stand facing away from the pulley machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with both hands, palms facing down, and bring your hands above your head.
Keep your upper arms close to your head and perpendicular to the floor.
Slowly lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable rope high pulley overhead tricep extension is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed using a cable machine with a rope attachment. It emphasizes triceps development and also engages the shoulders for stability.

exercises.name: cable supine reverse fly
exercises.notes: Attach a D-handle to a low pulley cable machine and lie face down on a flat bench.
instructions: Attach a D-handle to a low pulley cable machine and lie face down on a flat bench.
Grasp the D-handle with each hand, palms facing down, and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Keeping your arms straight, raise them out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable supine reverse fly is an isolation exercise targeting the rear deltoids, performed lying face down on a bench while using a cable machine. It emphasizes shoulder strength and stability, with secondary activation of the trapezius and rhomboids.

exercises.name: dumbbell rear lateral raise
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell rear lateral raise is an isolation exercise targeting the rear deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the traps and rhomboids. It is performed by hinging at the hips and raising dumbbells out to the sides, focusing on shoulder movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: cable shoulder press
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable machine so that the handles are at shoulder height.
instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the handles are at shoulder height.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and bring them up to shoulder level, with your elbows bent and pointing outwards.
Press the handles upwards until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable shoulder press is a compound exercise targeting the deltoid muscles using a cable machine. It involves pressing handles overhead from shoulder height, engaging the shoulders, triceps, and upper back.

exercises.name: dumbbell neutral grip bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Slowly lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell neutral grip bench press is a pressing exercise performed on a bench using dumbbells with palms facing each other. It primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated shoulder press
exercises.notes: Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated shoulder press is a strength exercise targeting the deltoid muscles of the shoulders. It is performed while seated, pressing dumbbells overhead to build shoulder and upper arm strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell full can lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell full can lateral raise is a shoulder exercise that targets the deltoids and also engages the traps and rotator cuff. It involves raising dumbbells to the sides with a slight bend in the elbows, mimicking the motion of pouring from a can, which helps reduce shoulder impingement risk.

exercises.name: dumbbell one arm seated hammer curl
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Rest your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
exercises.description: The dumbbell one arm seated hammer curl is an isolation exercise targeting the biceps and forearms. It is performed seated, with the elbow braced against the thigh, using a neutral grip to emphasize the brachialis and brachioradialis muscles.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated kickback
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight.
Bring your upper arms close to your sides and keep your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated kickback is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed while seated with dumbbells. It requires maintaining a stable posture and proper form to effectively engage the triceps.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated reverse grip one arm overhead tricep extension
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell with an underhand grip and extend your arm straight up overhead.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell with an underhand grip and extend your arm straight up overhead.
Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arm back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated reverse grip one arm overhead tricep extension is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed with a single dumbbell using an underhand grip while seated. It emphasizes triceps development and also engages the shoulders for stability.

exercises.name: dumbbell seated triceps extension
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up overhead.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up overhead.
Bend your elbows and lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your upper arms close to your ears.
Pause for a moment, then straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell seated triceps extension is an isolation exercise that targets the triceps, performed while seated and using a dumbbell held with both hands overhead.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Alternate Vertical Front Raises
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet shoulder‑width apart and a slight bend in the knees, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip).

exercises.name: Lever Seated Neutral grip Reverse Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles are about chest/shoulder level when seated and select an appropriate weight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Lever Reverse Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit at about shoulder level when seated and your back can rest fully against the pad.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit at about shoulder level when seated and your back can rest fully against the pad.
Sit upright with your chest up, feet flat on the floor, and brace your core; grasp the handles with a firm, neutral grip (as provided by the lever handles).
Position your elbows under or slightly in front of your wrists and keep the shoulder blades retracted and pressed into the pad for stability.
Exhale and press the handles upward and slightly forward by extending at the shoulders and elbows until your arms are nearly straight; avoid locking the elbows at the top.
Pause briefly at the top with control, then inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position at shoulder level, maintaining tension in the delts.
Keep the torso stable against the pad throughout the set—do not arch the lower back or use momentum to lift the weight.
Perform controlled repetitions and adjust load so you can maintain good form for the desired rep range.
exercises.description: Seated machine shoulder press performed on a lever apparatus with the back supported; the exercise presses the handles upward from shoulder level to primarily work the delts while the triceps assist and traps/upper back stabilize. Performed seated with the back against the pad and feet on the floor.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternate Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core (abs) braced to keep the torso stable.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core (abs) braced to keep the torso stable.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward, elbows bent and aligned slightly in front of the body.
Press one dumbbell straight overhead by extending the elbow and driving through the shoulder until the arm is nearly straight (avoid locking the elbow); exhale during the press.
Lower that dumbbell under control back to shoulder height while inhaling.
Immediately press the other dumbbell overhead in the same controlled manner, alternating arms with each repetition.
Keep the ribcage down and avoid excessive arching of the lower back; use the traps to stabilize the shoulders and maintain a neutral head position throughout.
Perform the movement in a slow, controlled rhythm and choose a weight that allows full range of motion without compensatory torso lean.
exercises.description: A standing alternating dumbbell shoulder press where one dumbbell is pressed overhead at a time; primarily develops the delts while the triceps assist and the traps and core stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to an incline of ~30-45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad and feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to an incline of ~30-45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad and feet planted on the floor.

Pick up a pair of dumbbells and hold them at shoulder height with a neutral to slightly forward grip (palms facing forward or slightly rotated), elbows roughly below or slightly in front of the shoulders.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades gently down and back to stabilize the upper back, and keep a slight natural arch in the spine without overarching.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled arc until your arms are nearly extended overhead (do not lock the elbows aggressively).
Pause briefly at the top with the weights balanced, keeping the shoulders and core engaged.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position, maintaining control and keeping the elbows from flaring excessively.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining steady tempo and avoiding momentum or excessive lower-back drive.
exercises.description: Seated incline dumbbell shoulder press performed on an inclined bench to emphasize the anterior delts while also recruiting the triceps and upper chest. Press the dumbbells overhead from shoulder height and lower with control.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Neutral Grip Front Raise
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle on a low cable pulley (near the floor) and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single-handle on a low cable pulley (near the floor) and select an appropriate weight.

Stand upright facing forward with feet hip-width apart and take a small step away from the machine so there is tension on the cable; hold the handle in one hand with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip at your side.
Keep a slight bend in the elbow and a neutral wrist; brace your core and maintain an upright torso (avoid leaning or rotating).
Exhale and raise the arm straight forward in a controlled motion to about shoulder height (arm roughly parallel to the floor), leading with the elbow but keeping the hand in line with the forearm.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping the shoulder down (do not shrug), then inhale and lower the arm slowly back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Focus on using the deltoid to lift rather than swinging the body or using momentum.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable front raise performed with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip to isolate the anterior deltoid while the cable provides constant tension. Performed standing, it emphasizes controlled shoulder flexion from the front of the body.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Alternate Vertical Front Raises
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet shoulder‑width apart and a slight bend in the knees, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip).
instructions: Stand upright with feet shoulder‑width apart and a slight bend in the knees, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip).
Keep your core braced (abs engaged) and shoulders down and back to avoid upper back rounding or low‑back arching.
Begin the movement by slightly bending the elbow of the working arm and lifting the dumbbell straight up in front of you in a vertical plane (thumb leading or neutral), keeping the wrist neutral.
Raise the weight in a controlled motion until the arm reaches shoulder level or just above (eye/ear height), avoiding momentum or body swing.
Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position under control.

Alternate arms, performing the same controlled lift on the opposite side while the other arm returns to the start; maintain an even tempo and steady breathing (exhale on the lift, inhale on the lower).
Do not use excessive weight that causes torso movement; if you arch your back or swing, reduce the load to preserve proper delt isolation.
exercises.description: A standing, single‑arm dumbbell front raise performed alternately to isolate the anterior deltoid while the opposite side rests. The exercise uses a controlled vertical lifting path to raise the weight in front of the body and slowly lower it back down.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Neutral grip Reverse Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles are about chest/shoulder level when seated and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles are about chest/shoulder level when seated and select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with feet flat on the floor, chest against the pad if present, and grab the handles with a neutral grip (palms facing inward).
Begin with arms extended forward (or slightly bent) so there is tension in the stack and a small bend at the elbows.
Exhale and pull the handles outward and back in a controlled arc, leading with the elbows until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and your shoulder blades are fully squeezed together.
Pause 1–2 seconds at the peak contraction while keeping the torso stable and avoiding backward lean or momentum.
Inhale and slowly return the handles to the starting position under control, maintaining the slight elbow bend throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; avoid shrugging the shoulders or using the lower back to drive the movement.
exercises.description: Seated neutral-grip reverse fly on a lever machine targets the posterior delts by moving the arms out to the sides while squeezing the shoulder blades; it also engages the upper back and traps for scapular retraction and stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Lever Reverse Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: dumbbell neutral grip bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Slowly lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell neutral grip bench press is a pressing exercise performed on a bench using dumbbells with palms facing each other. It primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders.

exercises.name: ez-bar close-grip bench press
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the ez barbell with a close grip, hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing forward.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Slowly lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The EZ-bar close-grip bench press is a compound upper body exercise that primarily targets the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It is performed lying on a bench, using a close grip on an EZ barbell to emphasize triceps activation.

exercises.name: cable triceps pushdown (v-bar)
exercises.notes: Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
instructions: Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Engage your triceps and exhale as you push the v-bar down until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your triceps.
Inhale as you slowly return the v-bar to the starting position, maintaining control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable triceps pushdown (v-bar) is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable machine and a v-bar attachment. It is commonly performed to build strength and muscle definition in the triceps.

exercises.name: dumbbell lying triceps extension
exercises.notes: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Lower the dumbbells down towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lying triceps extension is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed while lying on a bench and extending the arms with dumbbells.

exercises.name: cable close grip curl
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar to a low pulley cable machine.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a low pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Exhale and curl the bar up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The cable close grip curl is an isolation exercise targeting the biceps using a cable machine. It involves curling a straight bar attached to a low pulley with a close grip, emphasizing the biceps and forearms.

exercises.name: dumbbell alternate biceps curl
exercises.notes: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating arms.
exercises.description: The dumbbell alternate biceps curl is a classic arm exercise that targets the biceps and also engages the forearms. It involves curling dumbbells alternately while keeping the upper arms stationary, focusing on controlled movement and muscle contraction.

exercises.name: dumbbell lateral raise
exercises.notes: Keep your back straight and engage your core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell lateral raise is an isolation exercise that targets the deltoid muscles, particularly the lateral (side) head. It is commonly used to build shoulder width and strength.

exercises.name: dumbbell reverse fly
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Extend your arms straight down in front of you, palms facing each other.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: The dumbbell reverse fly is an exercise that targets the rear deltoids, with secondary emphasis on the trapezius and rhomboids. It is performed by hinging at the hips, keeping the back straight, and lifting dumbbells out to the sides in a controlled manner.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: dumbbell neutral grip bench press
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.
